Register a callback that is invoked with every incremental improvement to
objective values. The callback takes a model as argument.
The life-time of the model is limited to the callback so the
model has to be (deep) copied if it is to be used after the callback
Optimize API provides methods for solving using objective functions and weighted soft constraints

classes.name: z3.z3.ApplyResult

methods.name: z3.z3.ApplyResult.__len__
documentation:
Return the number of subgoals in `self`.

```pycon
>>> a, b = Ints('a b')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(Or(a == 0, a == 1), Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b)
>>> t = Tactic('split-clause')
>>> r = t(g)
>>> len(r)
2
>>> t = Then(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('split-clause'))
>>> len(t(g))
4
>>> t = Then(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('propagate-values'))
>>> len(t(g))
1
```

methods.name: z3.z3.ApplyResult.__getitem__
documentation:
Return one of the subgoals stored in ApplyResult object `self`.

```pycon
>>> a, b = Ints('a b')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(Or(a == 0, a == 1), Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b)
>>> t = Tactic('split-clause')
>>> r = t(g)
>>> r[0]
[a == 0, Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b]
>>> r[1]
[a == 1, Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b]
```

methods.name: z3.z3.ApplyResult.sexpr
documentation: Return a textual representation of the s-expression representing the set of subgoals in `self`.

methods.name: z3.z3.ApplyResult.as_expr
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression consisting of all subgoals.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 1)
>>> g.add(Or(x == 2, x == 3))
>>> r = Tactic('simplify')(g)
>>> r
[[Not(x <= 1), Or(x == 2, x == 3)]]
>>> r.as_expr()
And(Not(x <= 1), Or(x == 2, x == 3))
>>> r = Tactic('split-clause')(g)
>>> r
[[x > 1, x == 2], [x > 1, x == 3]]
>>> r.as_expr()
Or(And(x > 1, x == 2), And(x > 1, x == 3))
```

An ApplyResult object contains the subgoals produced by a tactic when applied to a goal.
It also contains model and proof converters.
ApplyResult

classes.name: z3.z3.Tactic

methods.name: z3.z3.Tactic.solver
documentation:
Create a solver using the tactic `self`.

The solver supports the methods `push()` and `pop()`, but it
will always solve each `check()` from scratch.

```pycon
>>> t = Then('simplify', 'nlsat')
>>> s = t.solver()
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> s.add(x**2 == 2, x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model()
[x = 1.4142135623?]
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Tactic.apply
documentation:
Apply tactic `self` to the given goal or Z3 Boolean expression using the given options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = Tactic('solve-eqs')
>>> t.apply(And(x == 0, y >= x + 1))
[[y >= 1]]
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Tactic.__call__
documentation:
Apply tactic `self` to the given goal or Z3 Boolean expression using the given options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = Tactic('solve-eqs')
>>> t(And(x == 0, y >= x + 1))
[[y >= 1]]
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Tactic.help
documentation: Display a string containing a description of the available options for the `self` tactic.

methods.name: z3.z3.Tactic.param_descrs
documentation: Return the parameter description set.

Tactics transform, solver and/or simplify sets of constraints (Goal).
A Tactic can be converted into a Solver using the method solver().

Several combinators are available for creating new tactics using the built-in ones:
Then(), OrElse(), FailIf(), Repeat(), When(), Cond().
Tactics

classes.name: z3.z3.Probe

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__lt__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is less than the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') < 10
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__gt__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is greater than the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') > 10
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
0.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__le__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is less than or equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') <= 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__ge__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is greater than or equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') >= 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__eq__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') == 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__ne__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is not equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') != 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
0.0
```

methods.name: z3.z3.Probe.__call__
documentation:
Evaluate the probe `self` in the given goal.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size')
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
2.0
>>> g.add(x < 20)
>>> p(g)
3.0
>>> p = Probe('num-consts')
>>> p(g)
1.0
>>> p = Probe('is-propositional')
>>> p(g)
0.0
>>> p = Probe('is-qflia')
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

Probes are used to inspect a goal (aka problem) and collect information that may be used
to decide which solver and/or preprocessing step will be used.

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.as_string
documentation: Return a Z3 floating point expression as a Python string.

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__add__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self + other`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = FP('y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> x + y
x + y
>>> (x + y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__radd__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other + self`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> 10 + x
1.25*(2**3) + x
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__sub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self - other`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = FP('y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> x - y
x - y
>>> (x - y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__rsub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other - self`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> 10 - x
1.25*(2**3) - x
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__mul__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self * other`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = FP('y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> x * y
x * y
>>> (x * y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
>>> 10 * y
1.25*(2**3) * y
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__rmul__
documentation:

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = FP('y', FPSort(8, 24))

x * y
>>> x * 10
x * 1.25*(2**3)
```

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__neg__

methods.name: z3.z3.FPRef.__div__

modules.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3
documentation:
Z3 is a high performance theorem prover developed at Microsoft Research.

Z3 is used in many applications such as: software/hardware verification and testing,
constraint solving, analysis of hybrid systems, security, biology (in silico analysis),
and geometrical problems.

Please send feedback, comments and/or corrections on the Issue tracker for
<https://github.com/Z3prover/z3.git>. Your comments are very valuable.

Small example:

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.add(x < 2)
>>> s.add(y == x + 1)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[x]
1
>>> m[y]
2
```

Z3 exceptions:

```pycon
>>> try:
...   x = BitVec('x', 32)
...   y = Bool('y')
...   # the expression x + y is type incorrect
...   n = x + y
... except Z3Exception as ex:
...   print("failed: %s" % ex)
failed: sort mismatch
```
Copyright (c) 2012 Microsoft Corporation
Z3 Python interface
Author: Leonardo de Moura (leonardo)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.open_log
documentation: Log interaction to a file. This function must be invoked immediately after init().

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.append_log
documentation: Append user-defined string to interaction log.

documentation: Convert an integer or string into a Z3 symbol.

documentation: Convert a Z3 symbol back into a Python object.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._get_args
documentation: Hack for having nary functions that can receive one argument that is the
list of arguments.
Use this when function takes a single list of arguments

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._get_args_ast_list
documentation: Use this when function takes multiple arguments

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.main_ctx
documentation:
Return a reference to the global Z3 context.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x.ctx == main_ctx()
True
>>> c = Context()
>>> c == main_ctx()
False
>>> x2 = Real('x', c)
>>> x2.ctx == c
True
>>> eq(x, x2)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.set_param
documentation:
Set Z3 global (or module) parameters.

```pycon
>>> set_param(precision=10)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.reset_params
documentation: Reset all global (or module) parameters.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.set_option
documentation: Alias for 'set_param' for backward compatibility.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.get_param
documentation:
Return the value of a Z3 global (or module) parameter

```pycon
>>> get_param('nlsat.reorder')
'true'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_ast
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an AST node.

```pycon
>>> is_ast(10)
False
>>> is_ast(IntVal(10))
True
>>> is_ast(Int('x'))
True
>>> is_ast(BoolSort())
True
>>> is_ast(Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort()))
True
>>> is_ast("x")
False
>>> is_ast(Solver())
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.eq
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` and `b` are structurally identical AST nodes.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> eq(x, y)
False
>>> eq(x + 1, x + 1)
True
>>> eq(x + 1, 1 + x)
False
>>> eq(simplify(x + 1), simplify(1 + x))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._sort_kind
documentation: Sorts

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_sort
documentation:
Return `True` if `s` is a Z3 sort.

```pycon
>>> is_sort(IntSort())
True
>>> is_sort(Int('x'))
False
>>> is_expr(Int('x'))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DeclareSort
documentation:
Create a new uninterpreted sort named `name`.

If `ctx=None`, then the new sort is declared in the global Z3Py context.

```pycon
>>> A = DeclareSort('A')
>>> a = Const('a', A)
>>> b = Const('b', A)
>>> a.sort() == A
True
>>> b.sort() == A
True
>>> a == b
a == b
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DeclareTypeVar
documentation:
Create a new type variable named `name`.

If `ctx=None`, then the new sort is declared in the global Z3Py context.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_func_decl
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 function declaration.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_func_decl(f)
True
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> is_func_decl(x)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Function
documentation:
Create a new Z3 uninterpreted function with the given sorts.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> f(f(0))
f(f(0))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FreshFunction
documentation: Create a new fresh Z3 uninterpreted function with the given sorts.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RecFunction
documentation: Create a new Z3 recursive with the given sorts.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RecAddDefinition
documentation:
Set the body of a recursive function.

: Recursive definitions can be simplified if they are applied to ground
  arguments.

```pycon
>>> ctx = Context()
>>> fac = RecFunction('fac', IntSort(ctx), IntSort(ctx))
>>> n = Int('n', ctx)
>>> RecAddDefinition(fac, n, If(n == 0, 1, n*fac(n-1)))
>>> simplify(fac(5))
120
>>> s = Solver(ctx=ctx)
>>> s.add(fac(n) < 3)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model().eval(fac(5))
120
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.deserialize
documentation:
inverse function to the serialize method on ExprRef.
It is made available to make it easier for users to serialize expressions back and forth between
strings. Solvers can be serialized using the 'sexpr()' method.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_expr
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 expression.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> is_expr(a)
True
>>> is_expr(a + 1)
True
>>> is_expr(IntSort())
False
>>> is_expr(1)
False
>>> is_expr(IntVal(1))
True
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_expr(ForAll(x, x >= 0))
True
>>> is_expr(FPVal(1.0))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_app
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 function application.

Note that, constants are function applications with 0 arguments.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> is_app(a)
True
>>> is_app(a + 1)
True
>>> is_app(IntSort())
False
>>> is_app(1)
False
>>> is_app(IntVal(1))
True
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_app(ForAll(x, x >= 0))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_const
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is Z3 constant/variable expression.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> is_const(a)
True
>>> is_const(a + 1)
False
>>> is_const(1)
False
>>> is_const(IntVal(1))
True
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_const(ForAll(x, x >= 0))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_var
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is variable.

Z3 uses de-Bruijn indices for representing bound variables in
quantifiers.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_var(x)
False
>>> is_const(x)
True
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> # Z3 replaces x with bound variables when ForAll is executed.
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == x)
>>> b = q.body()
>>> b
f(Var(0)) == Var(0)
>>> b.arg(1)
Var(0)
>>> is_var(b.arg(1))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.get_var_index
documentation:
Return the de-Bruijn index of the Z3 bounded variable `a`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> is_var(x)
False
>>> is_const(x)
True
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> # Z3 replaces x and y with bound variables when ForAll is executed.
>>> q = ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) == x + y)
>>> q.body()
f(Var(1), Var(0)) == Var(1) + Var(0)
>>> b = q.body()
>>> b.arg(0)
f(Var(1), Var(0))
>>> v1 = b.arg(0).arg(0)
>>> v2 = b.arg(0).arg(1)
>>> v1
Var(1)
>>> v2
Var(0)
>>> get_var_index(v1)
1
>>> get_var_index(v2)
0
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_app_of
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an application of the given kind `k`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> n = x + 1
>>> is_app_of(n, Z3_OP_ADD)
True
>>> is_app_of(n, Z3_OP_MUL)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.If
documentation:
Create a Z3 if-then-else expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> max = If(x > y, x, y)
>>> max
If(x > y, x, y)
>>> simplify(max)
If(x <= y, y, x)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Distinct
documentation:
Create a Z3 distinct expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> Distinct(x, y)
x != y
>>> z = Int('z')
>>> Distinct(x, y, z)
Distinct(x, y, z)
>>> simplify(Distinct(x, y, z))
Distinct(x, y, z)
>>> simplify(Distinct(x, y, z), blast_distinct=True)
And(Not(x == y), Not(x == z), Not(y == z))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Const
documentation:
Create a constant of the given sort.

```pycon
>>> Const('x', IntSort())
x
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Consts
documentation:
Create several constants of the given sort.

`names` is a string containing the names of all constants to be created.
Blank spaces separate the names of different constants.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = Consts('x y z', IntSort())
>>> x + y + z
x + y + z
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FreshConst
documentation: Create a fresh constant of a specified sort

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Var
documentation:
Create a Z3 free variable. Free variables are used to create quantified formulas.
A free variable with index n is bound when it occurs within the scope of n+1 quantified
declarations.

```pycon
>>> Var(0, IntSort())
Var(0)
>>> eq(Var(0, IntSort()), Var(0, BoolSort()))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RealVar
documentation:
Create a real free variable. Free variables are used to create quantified formulas.
They are also used to create polynomials.

```pycon
>>> RealVar(0)
Var(0)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RealVarVector
documentation:
Create a list of Real free variables.
The variables have ids: 0, 1, ..., n-1

```pycon
>>> x0, x1, x2, x3 = RealVarVector(4)
>>> x2
Var(2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_bool
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 Boolean expression.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> is_bool(p)
True
>>> q = Bool('q')
>>> is_bool(And(p, q))
True
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> is_bool(x)
False
>>> is_bool(x == 0)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_true
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is the Z3 true expression.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> is_true(p)
False
>>> is_true(simplify(p == p))
True
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> is_true(x == 0)
False
>>> # True is a Python Boolean expression
>>> is_true(True)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_false
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is the Z3 false expression.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> is_false(p)
False
>>> is_false(False)
False
>>> is_false(BoolVal(False))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_and
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 and expression.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> is_and(And(p, q))
True
>>> is_and(Or(p, q))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_or
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 or expression.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> is_or(Or(p, q))
True
>>> is_or(And(p, q))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_implies
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 implication expression.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> is_implies(Implies(p, q))
True
>>> is_implies(And(p, q))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_not
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 not expression.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> is_not(p)
False
>>> is_not(Not(p))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_eq
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 equality expression.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_eq(x == y)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_distinct
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 distinct expression.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = Ints('x y z')
>>> is_distinct(x == y)
False
>>> is_distinct(Distinct(x, y, z))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolSort
documentation:
Return the Boolean Z3 sort. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> BoolSort()
Bool
>>> p = Const('p', BoolSort())
>>> is_bool(p)
True
>>> r = Function('r', IntSort(), IntSort(), BoolSort())
>>> r(0, 1)
r(0, 1)
>>> is_bool(r(0, 1))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolVal
documentation:
Return the Boolean value `True` or `False`. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> BoolVal(True)
True
>>> is_true(BoolVal(True))
True
>>> is_true(True)
False
>>> is_false(BoolVal(False))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Bool
documentation:
Return a Boolean constant named `name`. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> q = Bool('q')
>>> And(p, q)
And(p, q)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Bools
documentation:
Return a tuple of Boolean constants.

`names` is a single string containing all names separated by blank spaces.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> p, q, r = Bools('p q r')
>>> And(p, Or(q, r))
And(p, Or(q, r))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolVector
documentation:
Return a list of Boolean constants of size `sz`.

The constants are named using the given prefix.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> P = BoolVector('p', 3)
>>> P
[p__0, p__1, p__2]
>>> And(P)
And(p__0, p__1, p__2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FreshBool
documentation:
Return a fresh Boolean constant in the given context using the given prefix.

If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> b1 = FreshBool()
>>> b2 = FreshBool()
>>> eq(b1, b2)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Implies
documentation:
Create a Z3 implies expression.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> Implies(p, q)
Implies(p, q)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Xor
documentation:
Create a Z3 Xor expression.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> Xor(p, q)
Xor(p, q)
>>> simplify(Xor(p, q))
Not(p == q)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Not
documentation:
Create a Z3 not expression or probe.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> Not(Not(p))
Not(Not(p))
>>> simplify(Not(Not(p)))
p
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._has_probe
documentation: Return `True` if one of the elements of the given collection is a Z3 probe.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.And
documentation:
Create a Z3 and-expression or and-probe.

```pycon
>>> p, q, r = Bools('p q r')
>>> And(p, q, r)
And(p, q, r)
>>> P = BoolVector('p', 5)
>>> And(P)
And(p__0, p__1, p__2, p__3, p__4)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Or
documentation:
Create a Z3 or-expression or or-probe.

```pycon
>>> p, q, r = Bools('p q r')
>>> Or(p, q, r)
Or(p, q, r)
>>> P = BoolVector('p', 5)
>>> Or(P)
Or(p__0, p__1, p__2, p__3, p__4)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_pattern
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 pattern (hint for quantifier instantiation.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0, patterns = [ f(x) ])
>>> q
ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.num_patterns()
1
>>> is_pattern(q.pattern(0))
True
>>> q.pattern(0)
f(Var(0))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.MultiPattern
documentation:
Create a Z3 multi-pattern using the given expressions `*args`

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> g = Function('g', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) != g(x), patterns = [ MultiPattern(f(x), g(x)) ])
>>> q
ForAll(x, f(x) != g(x))
>>> q.num_patterns()
1
>>> is_pattern(q.pattern(0))
True
>>> q.pattern(0)
MultiPattern(f(Var(0)), g(Var(0)))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_quantifier
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 quantifier.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> is_quantifier(q)
True
>>> is_quantifier(f(x))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ForAll
documentation:
Create a Z3 forall formula.

The parameters `weight`, `qid`, `skid`, `patterns` and `no_patterns` are optional annotations.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x, patterns=[ f(x, y) ])
ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x, weight=10)
ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Exists
documentation:
Create a Z3 exists formula.

The parameters `weight`, `qif`, `skid`, `patterns` and `no_patterns` are optional annotations.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> q = Exists([x, y], f(x, y) >= x, skid="foo")
>>> q
Exists([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> is_quantifier(q)
True
>>> r = Tactic('nnf')(q).as_expr()
>>> is_quantifier(r)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Lambda
documentation:
Create a Z3 lambda expression.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> mem0 = Array('mem0', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> lo, hi, e, i = Ints('lo hi e i')
>>> mem1 = Lambda([i], If(And(lo <= i, i <= hi), e, mem0[i]))
>>> mem1
Lambda(i, If(And(lo <= i, i <= hi), e, mem0[i]))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_arith_sort
documentation:
Return `True` if s is an arithmetical sort (type).

```pycon
>>> is_arith_sort(IntSort())
True
>>> is_arith_sort(RealSort())
True
>>> is_arith_sort(BoolSort())
False
>>> n = Int('x') + 1
>>> is_arith_sort(n.sort())
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_arith
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an arithmetical expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_arith(x)
True
>>> is_arith(x + 1)
True
>>> is_arith(1)
False
>>> is_arith(IntVal(1))
True
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> is_arith(y)
True
>>> is_arith(y + 1)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_int
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an integer expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_int(x + 1)
True
>>> is_int(1)
False
>>> is_int(IntVal(1))
True
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> is_int(y)
False
>>> is_int(y + 1)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_real
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a real expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_real(x + 1)
False
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> is_real(y)
True
>>> is_real(y + 1)
True
>>> is_real(1)
False
>>> is_real(RealVal(1))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_int_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an integer value of sort Int.

```pycon
>>> is_int_value(IntVal(1))
True
>>> is_int_value(1)
False
>>> is_int_value(Int('x'))
False
>>> n = Int('x') + 1
>>> n
x + 1
>>> n.arg(1)
1
>>> is_int_value(n.arg(1))
True
>>> is_int_value(RealVal("1/3"))
False
>>> is_int_value(RealVal(1))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_rational_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is rational value of sort Real.

```pycon
>>> is_rational_value(RealVal(1))
True
>>> is_rational_value(RealVal("3/5"))
True
>>> is_rational_value(IntVal(1))
False
>>> is_rational_value(1)
False
>>> n = Real('x') + 1
>>> n.arg(1)
1
>>> is_rational_value(n.arg(1))
True
>>> is_rational_value(Real('x'))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_algebraic_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an algebraic value of sort Real.

```pycon
>>> is_algebraic_value(RealVal("3/5"))
False
>>> n = simplify(Sqrt(2))
>>> n
1.4142135623?
>>> is_algebraic_value(n)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_add
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b + c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_add(x + y)
True
>>> is_add(x - y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_mul
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b * c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_mul(x * y)
True
>>> is_mul(x - y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_sub
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b - c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_sub(x - y)
True
>>> is_sub(x + y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_div
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b / c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Reals('x y')
>>> is_div(x / y)
True
>>> is_div(x + y)
False
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_div(x / y)
False
>>> is_idiv(x / y)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_idiv
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b div c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_idiv(x / y)
True
>>> is_idiv(x + y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_mod
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b % c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_mod(x % y)
True
>>> is_mod(x + y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_le
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b \<= c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_le(x <= y)
True
>>> is_le(x < y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_lt
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b \< c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_lt(x < y)
True
>>> is_lt(x == y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_ge
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b >= c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_ge(x >= y)
True
>>> is_ge(x == y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_gt
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form b > c.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> is_gt(x > y)
True
>>> is_gt(x == y)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_is_int
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form IsInt(b).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> is_is_int(IsInt(x))
True
>>> is_is_int(x)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_to_real
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form ToReal(b).

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> n = ToReal(x)
>>> n
ToReal(x)
>>> is_to_real(n)
True
>>> is_to_real(x)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_to_int
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is an expression of the form ToInt(b).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> n = ToInt(x)
>>> n
ToInt(x)
>>> is_to_int(n)
True
>>> is_to_int(x)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntSort
documentation:
Return the integer sort in the given context. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> IntSort()
Int
>>> x = Const('x', IntSort())
>>> is_int(x)
True
>>> x.sort() == IntSort()
True
>>> x.sort() == BoolSort()
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RealSort
documentation:
Return the real sort in the given context. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> RealSort()
Real
>>> x = Const('x', RealSort())
>>> is_real(x)
True
>>> is_int(x)
False
>>> x.sort() == RealSort()
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntVal
documentation:
Return a Z3 integer value. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> IntVal(1)
1
>>> IntVal("100")
100
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RealVal
documentation:
Return a Z3 real value.

`val` may be a Python int, long, float or string representing a number in decimal or rational notation.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> RealVal(1)
1
>>> RealVal(1).sort()
Real
>>> RealVal("3/5")
3/5
>>> RealVal("1.5")
3/2
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatVal
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational a/b.

If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> RatVal(3,5)
3/5
>>> RatVal(3,5).sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Q
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational a/b.

If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> Q(3,5)
3/5
>>> Q(3,5).sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Int
documentation:
Return an integer constant named `name`. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> is_int(x)
True
>>> is_int(x + 1)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Ints
documentation:
Return a tuple of Integer constants.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = Ints('x y z')
>>> Sum(x, y, z)
x + y + z
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntVector
documentation:
Return a list of integer constants of size `sz`.

```pycon
>>> X = IntVector('x', 3)
>>> X
[x__0, x__1, x__2]
>>> Sum(X)
x__0 + x__1 + x__2
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FreshInt
documentation:
Return a fresh integer constant in the given context using the given prefix.

```pycon
>>> x = FreshInt()
>>> y = FreshInt()
>>> eq(x, y)
False
>>> x.sort()
Int
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Real
documentation:
Return a real constant named `name`. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> is_real(x)
True
>>> is_real(x + 1)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Reals
documentation:
Return a tuple of real constants.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = Reals('x y z')
>>> Sum(x, y, z)
x + y + z
>>> Sum(x, y, z).sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RealVector
documentation:
Return a list of real constants of size `sz`.

```pycon
>>> X = RealVector('x', 3)
>>> X
[x__0, x__1, x__2]
>>> Sum(X)
x__0 + x__1 + x__2
>>> Sum(X).sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FreshReal
documentation:
Return a fresh real constant in the given context using the given prefix.

```pycon
>>> x = FreshReal()
>>> y = FreshReal()
>>> eq(x, y)
False
>>> x.sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ToReal
documentation:
Return the Z3 expression ToReal(a).

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> x.sort()
Int
>>> n = ToReal(x)
>>> n
ToReal(x)
>>> n.sort()
Real
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ToInt
documentation:
Return the Z3 expression ToInt(a).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x.sort()
Real
>>> n = ToInt(x)
>>> n
ToInt(x)
>>> n.sort()
Int
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IsInt
documentation:
Return the Z3 predicate IsInt(a).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> IsInt(x + "1/2")
IsInt(x + 1/2)
>>> solve(IsInt(x + "1/2"), x > 0, x < 1)
[x = 1/2]
>>> solve(IsInt(x + "1/2"), x > 0, x < 1, x != "1/2")
no solution
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Sqrt
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression which represents the square root of a.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> Sqrt(x)
x**(1/2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Cbrt
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression which represents the cubic root of a.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> Cbrt(x)
x**(1/3)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_bv_sort
documentation:
Return True if `s` is a Z3 bit-vector sort.

```pycon
>>> is_bv_sort(BitVecSort(32))
True
>>> is_bv_sort(IntSort())
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_bv
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 bit-vector expression.

```pycon
>>> b = BitVec('b', 32)
>>> is_bv(b)
True
>>> is_bv(b + 10)
True
>>> is_bv(Int('x'))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_bv_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 bit-vector numeral value.

```pycon
>>> b = BitVec('b', 32)
>>> is_bv_value(b)
False
>>> b = BitVecVal(10, 32)
>>> b
10
>>> is_bv_value(b)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BV2Int
documentation:
Return the Z3 expression BV2Int(a).

```pycon
>>> b = BitVec('b', 3)
>>> BV2Int(b).sort()
Int
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> x > BV2Int(b)
x > BV2Int(b)
>>> x > BV2Int(b, is_signed=False)
x > BV2Int(b)
>>> x > BV2Int(b, is_signed=True)
x > If(b < 0, BV2Int(b) - 8, BV2Int(b))
>>> solve(x > BV2Int(b), b == 1, x < 3)
[x = 2, b = 1]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Int2BV
documentation:
Return the z3 expression Int2BV(a, num_bits).
It is a bit-vector of width num_bits and represents the
modulo of a by 2^num_bits

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecSort
documentation:
Return a Z3 bit-vector sort of the given size. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> Byte = BitVecSort(8)
>>> Word = BitVecSort(16)
>>> Byte
BitVec(8)
>>> x = Const('x', Byte)
>>> eq(x, BitVec('x', 8))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecVal
documentation:
Return a bit-vector value with the given number of bits. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> v = BitVecVal(10, 32)
>>> v
10
>>> print("0x%.8x" % v.as_long())
0x0000000a
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVec
documentation:
Return a bit-vector constant named `name`. `bv` may be the number of bits of a bit-vector sort.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> x  = BitVec('x', 16)
>>> is_bv(x)
True
>>> x.size()
16
>>> x.sort()
BitVec(16)
>>> word = BitVecSort(16)
>>> x2 = BitVec('x', word)
>>> eq(x, x2)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecs
documentation:
Return a tuple of bit-vector constants of size bv.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = BitVecs('x y z', 16)
>>> x.size()
16
>>> x.sort()
BitVec(16)
>>> Sum(x, y, z)
0 + x + y + z
>>> Product(x, y, z)
1*x*y*z
>>> simplify(Product(x, y, z))
x*y*z
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Concat
documentation:
Create a Z3 bit-vector concatenation expression.

```pycon
>>> v = BitVecVal(1, 4)
>>> Concat(v, v+1, v)
Concat(Concat(1, 1 + 1), 1)
>>> simplify(Concat(v, v+1, v))
289
>>> print("%.3x" % simplify(Concat(v, v+1, v)).as_long())
121
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Extract
documentation:
Create a Z3 bit-vector extraction expression.
Extract is overloaded to also work on sequence extraction.
The functions SubString and SubSeq are redirected to Extract.
For this case, the arguments are reinterpreted as:

> high - is a sequence (string)
> low  - is an offset
> a    - is the length to be extracted

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 8)
>>> Extract(6, 2, x)
Extract(6, 2, x)
>>> Extract(6, 2, x).sort()
BitVec(5)
>>> simplify(Extract(StringVal("abcd"),2,1))
"c"
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ULE
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) `other <= self`.

Use the operator \<= for signed less than or equal to.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> ULE(x, y)
ULE(x, y)
>>> (x <= y).sexpr()
'(bvsle x y)'
>>> ULE(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvule x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ULT
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) `other < self`.

Use the operator \< for signed less than.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> ULT(x, y)
ULT(x, y)
>>> (x < y).sexpr()
'(bvslt x y)'
>>> ULT(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvult x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.UGE
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) `other >= self`.

Use the operator >= for signed greater than or equal to.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> UGE(x, y)
UGE(x, y)
>>> (x >= y).sexpr()
'(bvsge x y)'
>>> UGE(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvuge x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.UGT
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) `other > self`.

Use the operator > for signed greater than.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> UGT(x, y)
UGT(x, y)
>>> (x > y).sexpr()
'(bvsgt x y)'
>>> UGT(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvugt x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.UDiv
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) division `self / other`.

Use the operator / for signed division.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> UDiv(x, y)
UDiv(x, y)
>>> UDiv(x, y).sort()
BitVec(32)
>>> (x / y).sexpr()
'(bvsdiv x y)'
>>> UDiv(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvudiv x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.URem
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (unsigned) remainder `self % other`.

Use the operator % for signed modulus, and SRem() for signed remainder.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> URem(x, y)
URem(x, y)
>>> URem(x, y).sort()
BitVec(32)
>>> (x % y).sexpr()
'(bvsmod x y)'
>>> URem(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvurem x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SRem
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression signed remainder.

Use the operator % for signed modulus, and URem() for unsigned remainder.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> SRem(x, y)
SRem(x, y)
>>> SRem(x, y).sort()
BitVec(32)
>>> (x % y).sexpr()
'(bvsmod x y)'
>>> SRem(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvsrem x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.LShR
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression logical right shift.

Use the operator >> for the arithmetical right shift.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> LShR(x, y)
LShR(x, y)
>>> (x >> y).sexpr()
'(bvashr x y)'
>>> LShR(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvlshr x y)'
>>> BitVecVal(4, 3)
4
>>> BitVecVal(4, 3).as_signed_long()
-4
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(4, 3) >> 1).as_signed_long()
-2
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(4, 3) >> 1)
6
>>> simplify(LShR(BitVecVal(4, 3), 1))
2
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(2, 3) >> 1)
1
>>> simplify(LShR(BitVecVal(2, 3), 1))
1
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RotateLeft
documentation:
Return an expression representing `a` rotated to the left `b` times.

```pycon
>>> a, b = BitVecs('a b', 16)
>>> RotateLeft(a, b)
RotateLeft(a, b)
>>> simplify(RotateLeft(a, 0))
a
>>> simplify(RotateLeft(a, 16))
a
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RotateRight
documentation:
Return an expression representing `a` rotated to the right `b` times.

```pycon
>>> a, b = BitVecs('a b', 16)
>>> RotateRight(a, b)
RotateRight(a, b)
>>> simplify(RotateRight(a, 0))
a
>>> simplify(RotateRight(a, 16))
a
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SignExt
documentation:
Return a bit-vector expression with `n` extra sign-bits.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 16)
>>> n = SignExt(8, x)
>>> n.size()
24
>>> n
SignExt(8, x)
>>> n.sort()
BitVec(24)
>>> v0 = BitVecVal(2, 2)
>>> v0
2
>>> v0.size()
2
>>> v  = simplify(SignExt(6, v0))
>>> v
254
>>> v.size()
8
>>> print("%.x" % v.as_long())
fe
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ZeroExt
documentation:
Return a bit-vector expression with `n` extra zero-bits.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 16)
>>> n = ZeroExt(8, x)
>>> n.size()
24
>>> n
ZeroExt(8, x)
>>> n.sort()
BitVec(24)
>>> v0 = BitVecVal(2, 2)
>>> v0
2
>>> v0.size()
2
>>> v  = simplify(ZeroExt(6, v0))
>>> v
2
>>> v.size()
8
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RepeatBitVec
documentation:
Return an expression representing `n` copies of `a`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 8)
>>> n = RepeatBitVec(4, x)
>>> n
RepeatBitVec(4, x)
>>> n.size()
32
>>> v0 = BitVecVal(10, 4)
>>> print("%.x" % v0.as_long())
a
>>> v = simplify(RepeatBitVec(4, v0))
>>> v.size()
16
>>> print("%.x" % v.as_long())
aaaa
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVRedAnd
documentation: Return the reduction-and expression of `a`.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVRedOr
documentation: Return the reduction-or expression of `a`.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVAddNoOverflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector addition does not overflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVAddNoUnderflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that signed bit-vector addition does not underflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVSubNoOverflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector subtraction does not overflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVSubNoUnderflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector subtraction does not underflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVSDivNoOverflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector signed division does not overflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVSNegNoOverflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector unary negation does not overflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVMulNoOverflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector multiplication does not overflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BVMulNoUnderflow
documentation: A predicate the determines that bit-vector signed multiplication does not underflow

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_array
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 array expression.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_array(a)
True
>>> is_array(Store(a, 0, 1))
True
>>> is_array(a[0])
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_const_array
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 constant array.

```pycon
>>> a = K(IntSort(), 10)
>>> is_const_array(a)
True
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_const_array(a)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_K
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 constant array.

```pycon
>>> a = K(IntSort(), 10)
>>> is_K(a)
True
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_K(a)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_map
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 map array expression.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> b = Array('b', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a  = Map(f, b)
>>> a
Map(f, b)
>>> is_map(a)
True
>>> is_map(b)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_default
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 default array expression.
\>>> d = Default(K(IntSort(), 10))
\>>> is_default(d)
True

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.get_map_func
documentation:
Return the function declaration associated with a Z3 map array expression.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> b = Array('b', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a  = Map(f, b)
>>> eq(f, get_map_func(a))
True
>>> get_map_func(a)
f
>>> get_map_func(a)(0)
f(0)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArraySort
documentation:
Return the Z3 array sort with the given domain and range sorts.

```pycon
>>> A = ArraySort(IntSort(), BoolSort())
>>> A
Array(Int, Bool)
>>> A.domain()
Int
>>> A.range()
Bool
>>> AA = ArraySort(IntSort(), A)
>>> AA
Array(Int, Array(Int, Bool))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Array
documentation:
Return an array constant named `name` with the given domain and range sorts.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a.sort()
Array(Int, Int)
>>> a[0]
a[0]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Update
documentation:
Return a Z3 store array expression.

```pycon
>>> a    = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> i, v = Ints('i v')
>>> s    = Update(a, i, v)
>>> s.sort()
Array(Int, Int)
>>> prove(s[i] == v)
proved
>>> j    = Int('j')
>>> prove(Implies(i != j, s[j] == a[j]))
proved
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Default
documentation:
Return a default value for array expression.
\>>> b = K(IntSort(), 1)
\>>> prove(Default(b) == 1)
proved

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Store
documentation:
Return a Z3 store array expression.

```pycon
>>> a    = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> i, v = Ints('i v')
>>> s    = Store(a, i, v)
>>> s.sort()
Array(Int, Int)
>>> prove(s[i] == v)
proved
>>> j    = Int('j')
>>> prove(Implies(i != j, s[j] == a[j]))
proved
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Select
documentation:
Return a Z3 select array expression.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> i = Int('i')
>>> Select(a, i)
a[i]
>>> eq(Select(a, i), a[i])
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Map
documentation:
Return a Z3 map array expression.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a1 = Array('a1', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a2 = Array('a2', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> b  = Map(f, a1, a2)
>>> b
Map(f, a1, a2)
>>> prove(b[0] == f(a1[0], a2[0]))
proved
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.K
documentation:
Return a Z3 constant array expression.

```pycon
>>> a = K(IntSort(), 10)
>>> a
K(Int, 10)
>>> a.sort()
Array(Int, Int)
>>> i = Int('i')
>>> a[i]
K(Int, 10)[i]
>>> simplify(a[i])
10
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Ext
documentation:
Return extensionality index for one-dimensional arrays.
>> a, b = Consts('a b', SetSort(IntSort()))
>> Ext(a, b)
Ext(a, b)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_select
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 array select application.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_select(a)
False
>>> i = Int('i')
>>> is_select(a[i])
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_store
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 array store application.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> is_store(a)
False
>>> is_store(Store(a, 0, 1))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetSort
documentation: Create a set sort over element sort s
Sets

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.EmptySet
documentation:
Create the empty set
\>>> EmptySet(IntSort())
K(Int, False)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FullSet
documentation:
Create the full set
\>>> FullSet(IntSort())
K(Int, True)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetUnion
documentation:
Take the union of sets
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> b = Const('b', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetUnion(a, b)
union(a, b)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetIntersect
documentation:
Take the union of sets
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> b = Const('b', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetIntersect(a, b)
intersection(a, b)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetAdd
documentation:
Add element e to set s
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetAdd(a, 1)
Store(a, 1, True)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetDel
documentation:
Remove element e to set s
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetDel(a, 1)
Store(a, 1, False)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetComplement
documentation:
The complement of set s
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetComplement(a)
complement(a)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SetDifference
documentation:
The set difference of a and b
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> b = Const('b', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> SetDifference(a, b)
setminus(a, b)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IsMember
documentation:
Check if e is a member of set s
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> IsMember(1, a)
a\[1\]

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IsSubset
documentation:
Check if a is a subset of b
\>>> a = Const('a', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> b = Const('b', SetSort(IntSort()))
\>>> IsSubset(a, b)
subset(a, b)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._valid_accessor
documentation: Return `True` if acc is pair of the form (String, Datatype or Sort).
Datatypes

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.CreateDatatypes
documentation:
Create mutually recursive Z3 datatypes using 1 or more Datatype helper objects.

In the following example we define a Tree-List using two mutually recursive datatypes.

```pycon
>>> TreeList = Datatype('TreeList')
>>> Tree     = Datatype('Tree')
>>> # Tree has two constructors: leaf and node
>>> Tree.declare('leaf', ('val', IntSort()))
>>> # a node contains a list of trees
>>> Tree.declare('node', ('children', TreeList))
>>> TreeList.declare('nil')
>>> TreeList.declare('cons', ('car', Tree), ('cdr', TreeList))
>>> Tree, TreeList = CreateDatatypes(Tree, TreeList)
>>> Tree.val(Tree.leaf(10))
val(leaf(10))
>>> simplify(Tree.val(Tree.leaf(10)))
10
>>> n1 = Tree.node(TreeList.cons(Tree.leaf(10), TreeList.cons(Tree.leaf(20), TreeList.nil)))
>>> n1
node(cons(leaf(10), cons(leaf(20), nil)))
>>> n2 = Tree.node(TreeList.cons(n1, TreeList.nil))
>>> simplify(n2 == n1)
False
>>> simplify(TreeList.car(Tree.children(n2)) == n1)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSort
documentation: Create a reference to a sort that was declared, or will be declared, as a recursive datatype

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.TupleSort
documentation:
Create a named tuple sort base on a set of underlying sorts
Example:
    >>> pair, mk_pair, (first, second) = TupleSort("pair", [IntSort(), StringSort()])

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DisjointSum
documentation:
Create a named tagged union sort base on a set of underlying sorts
Example:
    >>> sum, ((inject0, extract0), (inject1, extract1)) = DisjointSum("+", [IntSort(), StringSort()])

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.EnumSort
documentation:
Return a new enumeration sort named `name` containing the given values.

The result is a pair (sort, list of constants).
Example:
    >>> Color, (red, green, blue) = EnumSort('Color', ['red', 'green', 'blue'])

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.args2params
documentation:
Convert python arguments into a Z3_params object.
A ':' is added to the keywords, and '\_' is replaced with '-'

```pycon
>>> args2params(['model', True, 'relevancy', 2], {'elim_and' : True})
(params model true relevancy 2 elim_and true)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_as_array
documentation: Return true if n is a Z3 expression of the form (_ as-array f).

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.get_as_array_func
documentation: Return the function declaration f associated with a Z3 expression of the form (_ as-array f).

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SolverFor
documentation:
Create a solver customized for the given logic.

The parameter `logic` is a string. It should be contains
the name of a SMT-LIB logic.
See <http://www.smtlib.org/> for the name of all available logics.

```pycon
>>> s = SolverFor("QF_LIA")
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.add(x < 2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model()
[x = 1]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SimpleSolver
documentation:
Return a simple general purpose solver with limited amount of preprocessing.

```pycon
>>> s = SimpleSolver()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FiniteDomainSort
documentation: Create a named finite domain sort of a given size sz

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_finite_domain_sort
documentation:
Return True if `s` is a Z3 finite-domain sort.

```pycon
>>> is_finite_domain_sort(FiniteDomainSort('S', 100))
True
>>> is_finite_domain_sort(IntSort())
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_finite_domain
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 finite-domain expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FiniteDomainSort('S', 100)
>>> b = Const('b', s)
>>> is_finite_domain(b)
True
>>> is_finite_domain(Int('x'))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FiniteDomainVal
documentation:
Return a Z3 finite-domain value. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> s = FiniteDomainSort('S', 256)
>>> FiniteDomainVal(255, s)
255
>>> FiniteDomainVal('100', s)
100
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_finite_domain_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 finite-domain value.

```pycon
>>> s = FiniteDomainSort('S', 100)
>>> b = Const('b', s)
>>> is_finite_domain_value(b)
False
>>> b = FiniteDomainVal(10, s)
>>> b
10
>>> is_finite_domain_value(b)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AndThen
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies the tactics in `*ts` in sequence.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = AndThen(Tactic('simplify'), Tactic('solve-eqs'))
>>> t(And(x == 0, y > x + 1))
[[Not(y <= 1)]]
>>> t(And(x == 0, y > x + 1)).as_expr()
Not(y <= 1)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Then
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies the tactics in `*ts` in sequence. Shorthand for AndThen(ts, ks).

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = Then(Tactic('simplify'), Tactic('solve-eqs'))
>>> t(And(x == 0, y > x + 1))
[[Not(y <= 1)]]
>>> t(And(x == 0, y > x + 1)).as_expr()
Not(y <= 1)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.OrElse
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies the tactics in `*ts` until one of them succeeds (it doesn't fail).

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> t = OrElse(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('skip'))
>>> # Tactic split-clause fails if there is no clause in the given goal.
>>> t(x == 0)
[[x == 0]]
>>> t(Or(x == 0, x == 1))
[[x == 0], [x == 1]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ParOr
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies the tactics in `*ts` in parallel until one of them succeeds (it doesn't fail).

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> t = ParOr(Tactic('simplify'), Tactic('fail'))
>>> t(x + 1 == 2)
[[x == 1]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ParThen
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies t1 and then t2 to every subgoal produced by t1.
The subgoals are processed in parallel.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = ParThen(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('propagate-values'))
>>> t(And(Or(x == 1, x == 2), y == x + 1))
[[x == 1, y == 2], [x == 2, y == 3]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ParAndThen
documentation: Alias for ParThen(t1, t2, ctx).

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.With
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies tactic `t` using the given configuration options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = With(Tactic('simplify'), som=True)
>>> t((x + 1)*(y + 2) == 0)
[[2*x + y + x*y == -2]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.WithParams
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies tactic `t` using the given configuration options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> p = ParamsRef()
>>> p.set("som", True)
>>> t = WithParams(Tactic('simplify'), p)
>>> t((x + 1)*(y + 2) == 0)
[[2*x + y + x*y == -2]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Repeat
documentation:
Return a tactic that keeps applying `t` until the goal is not modified anymore
or the maximum number of iterations `max` is reached.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> c = And(Or(x == 0, x == 1), Or(y == 0, y == 1), x > y)
>>> t = Repeat(OrElse(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('skip')))
>>> r = t(c)
>>> for subgoal in r: print(subgoal)
[x == 0, y == 0, x > y]
[x == 0, y == 1, x > y]
[x == 1, y == 0, x > y]
[x == 1, y == 1, x > y]
>>> t = Then(t, Tactic('propagate-values'))
>>> t(c)
[[x == 1, y == 0]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.TryFor
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies `t` to a given goal for `ms` milliseconds.

If `t` does not terminate in `ms` milliseconds, then it fails.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.tactics
documentation:
Return a list of all available tactics in Z3.

```pycon
>>> l = tactics()
>>> l.count('simplify') == 1
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.tactic_description
documentation:
Return a short description for the tactic named `name`.

```pycon
>>> d = tactic_description('simplify')
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.describe_tactics
documentation: Display a (tabular) description of all available tactics in Z3.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_probe
documentation:
Return `True` if `p` is a Z3 probe.

```pycon
>>> is_probe(Int('x'))
False
>>> is_probe(Probe('memory'))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.probes
documentation:
Return a list of all available probes in Z3.

```pycon
>>> l = probes()
>>> l.count('memory') == 1
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.probe_description
documentation:
Return a short description for the probe named `name`.

```pycon
>>> d = probe_description('memory')
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.describe_probes
documentation: Display a (tabular) description of all available probes in Z3.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FailIf
documentation:
Return a tactic that fails if the probe `p` evaluates to true.
Otherwise, it returns the input goal unmodified.

In the following example, the tactic applies 'simplify' if and only if there are
more than 2 constraints in the goal.

```pycon
>>> t = OrElse(FailIf(Probe('size') > 2), Tactic('simplify'))
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(y > 0)
>>> t(g)
[[x > 0, y > 0]]
>>> g.add(x == y + 1)
>>> t(g)
[[Not(x <= 0), Not(y <= 0), x == 1 + y]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.When
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies tactic `t` only if probe `p` evaluates to true.
Otherwise, it returns the input goal unmodified.

```pycon
>>> t = When(Probe('size') > 2, Tactic('simplify'))
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(y > 0)
>>> t(g)
[[x > 0, y > 0]]
>>> g.add(x == y + 1)
>>> t(g)
[[Not(x <= 0), Not(y <= 0), x == 1 + y]]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Cond
documentation:
Return a tactic that applies tactic `t1` to a goal if probe `p` evaluates to true, and `t2` otherwise.

```pycon
>>> t = Cond(Probe('is-qfnra'), Tactic('qfnra'), Tactic('smt'))
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.simplify
documentation:
Simplify the expression `a` using the given options.

This function has many options. Use `help_simplify` to obtain the complete list.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> simplify(x + 1 + y + x + 1)
2 + 2*x + y
>>> simplify((x + 1)*(y + 1), som=True)
1 + x + y + x*y
>>> simplify(Distinct(x, y, 1), blast_distinct=True)
And(Not(x == y), Not(x == 1), Not(y == 1))
>>> simplify(And(x == 0, y == 1), elim_and=True)
Not(Or(Not(x == 0), Not(y == 1)))
```
Utils

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.help_simplify
documentation: Return a string describing all options available for Z3 `simplify` procedure.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.simplify_param_descrs
documentation: Return the set of parameter descriptions for Z3 `simplify` procedure.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.substitute
documentation:
Apply substitution m on t, m is a list of pairs of the form (from, to).
Every occurrence in t of from is replaced with to.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> substitute(x + 1, (x, y + 1))
y + 1 + 1
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> substitute(f(x) + f(y), (f(x), IntVal(1)), (f(y), IntVal(1)))
1 + 1
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.substitute_vars
documentation:
Substitute the free variables in t with the expression in m.

```pycon
>>> v0 = Var(0, IntSort())
>>> v1 = Var(1, IntSort())
>>> x  = Int('x')
>>> f  = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> # replace v0 with x+1 and v1 with x
>>> substitute_vars(f(v0, v1), x + 1, x)
f(x + 1, x)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.substitute_funs
documentation:
Apply substitution m on t, m is a list of pairs of a function and expression (from, to)
Every occurrence in to of the function from is replaced with the expression to.
The expression to can have free variables, that refer to the arguments of from.
For examples, see

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Sum
documentation:
Create the sum of the Z3 expressions.

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Ints('a b c')
>>> Sum(a, b, c)
a + b + c
>>> Sum([a, b, c])
a + b + c
>>> A = IntVector('a', 5)
>>> Sum(A)
a__0 + a__1 + a__2 + a__3 + a__4
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Product
documentation:
Create the product of the Z3 expressions.

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Ints('a b c')
>>> Product(a, b, c)
a*b*c
>>> Product([a, b, c])
a*b*c
>>> A = IntVector('a', 5)
>>> Product(A)
a__0*a__1*a__2*a__3*a__4
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Abs
documentation: Create the absolute value of an arithmetic expression

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AtMost
documentation:

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Bools('a b c')
>>> f = AtMost(a, b, c, 2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AtLeast
documentation:
Create an at-least Pseudo-Boolean k constraint.

```pycon

>>> f = AtLeast(a, b, c, 2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.PbLe
documentation:
Create a Pseudo-Boolean inequality k constraint.

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Bools('a b c')
>>> f = PbLe(((a,1),(b,3),(c,2)), 3)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.PbGe
documentation:
Create a Pseudo-Boolean inequality k constraint.

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Bools('a b c')
>>> f = PbGe(((a,1),(b,3),(c,2)), 3)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.PbEq
documentation:
Create a Pseudo-Boolean equality k constraint.

```pycon
>>> a, b, c = Bools('a b c')
>>> f = PbEq(((a,1),(b,3),(c,2)), 3)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.solve
documentation:
Solve the constraints `*args`.

This is a simple function for creating demonstrations. It creates a solver,
configure it using the options in `keywords`, adds the constraints
in `args`, and invokes check.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> solve(a > 0, a < 2)
[a = 1]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.solve_using
documentation:
Solve the constraints `*args` using solver `s`.

This is a simple function for creating demonstrations. It is similar to `solve`,
but it uses the given solver `s`.
It configures solver `s` using the options in `keywords`, adds the constraints
in `args`, and invokes check.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.prove
documentation:
Try to prove the given claim.

This is a simple function for creating demonstrations.  It tries to prove
`claim` by showing the negation is unsatisfiable.

```pycon
>>> p, q = Bools('p q')
>>> prove(Not(And(p, q)) == Or(Not(p), Not(q)))
proved
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._solve_html
documentation: Version of function `solve` that renders HTML output.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._solve_using_html
documentation: Version of function `solve_using` that renders HTML.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3._prove_html

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.parse_smt2_string
documentation:
Parse a string in SMT 2.0 format using the given sorts and decls.

The arguments sorts and decls are Python dictionaries used to initialize
the symbol table used for the SMT 2.0 parser.

```pycon
>>> parse_smt2_string('(declare-const x Int) (assert (> x 0)) (assert (< x 10))')
[x > 0, x < 10]
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> parse_smt2_string('(assert (> (+ foo (g bar)) 0))', decls={ 'foo' : x, 'bar' : y, 'g' : f})
[x + f(y) > 0]
>>> parse_smt2_string('(declare-const a U) (assert (> a 0))', sorts={ 'U' : IntSort() })
[a > 0]
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.parse_smt2_file
documentation:
Parse a file in SMT 2.0 format using the given sorts and decls.

This function is similar to parse_smt2_string().

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Float32

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fp_sort
documentation:
Return True if `s` is a Z3 floating-point sort.

```pycon
>>> is_fp_sort(FPSort(8, 24))
True
>>> is_fp_sort(IntSort())
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fprm_sort
documentation:
Return True if `s` is a Z3 floating-point rounding mode sort.

```pycon
>>> is_fprm_sort(FPSort(8, 24))
False
>>> is_fprm_sort(RNE().sort())
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fprm
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 floating-point rounding mode expression.

```pycon
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> is_fprm(rm)
True
>>> rm = 1.0
>>> is_fprm(rm)
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fprm_value
documentation: Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 floating-point rounding mode numeral value.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fp
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 floating-point expression.

```pycon
>>> b = FP('b', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> is_fp(b)
True
>>> is_fp(b + 1.0)
True
>>> is_fp(Int('x'))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.is_fp_value
documentation:
Return `True` if `a` is a Z3 floating-point numeral value.

```pycon
>>> b = FP('b', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> is_fp_value(b)
False
>>> b = FPVal(1.0, FPSort(8, 24))
>>> b
1
>>> is_fp_value(b)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FPSort
documentation:
Return a Z3 floating-point sort of the given sizes. If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> Single = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> Double = FPSort(11, 53)
>>> Single
FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x = Const('x', Single)
>>> eq(x, FP('x', FPSort(8, 24)))
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpNaN
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point NaN term.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> set_fpa_pretty(True)
>>> fpNaN(s)
NaN
>>> pb = get_fpa_pretty()
>>> set_fpa_pretty(False)
>>> fpNaN(s)
fpNaN(FPSort(8, 24))
>>> set_fpa_pretty(pb)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpPlusInfinity
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point +oo term.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> pb = get_fpa_pretty()
>>> set_fpa_pretty(True)
>>> fpPlusInfinity(s)
+oo
>>> set_fpa_pretty(False)
>>> fpPlusInfinity(s)
fpPlusInfinity(FPSort(8, 24))
>>> set_fpa_pretty(pb)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpMinusInfinity
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point -oo term.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpInfinity
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point +oo or -oo term.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpPlusZero
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point +0.0 term.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpMinusZero
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point -0.0 term.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpZero
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point +0.0 or -0.0 term.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FPVal
documentation:
Return a floating-point value of value `val` and sort `fps`.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> v = FPVal(20.0, FPSort(8, 24))
>>> v
1.25*(2**4)
>>> print("0x%.8x" % v.exponent_as_long(False))
0x00000004
>>> v = FPVal(2.25, FPSort(8, 24))
>>> v
1.125*(2**1)
>>> v = FPVal(-2.25, FPSort(8, 24))
>>> v
-1.125*(2**1)
>>> FPVal(-0.0, FPSort(8, 24))
-0.0
>>> FPVal(0.0, FPSort(8, 24))
+0.0
>>> FPVal(+0.0, FPSort(8, 24))
+0.0
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FP
documentation:
Return a floating-point constant named `name`.
`fpsort` is the floating-point sort.
If `ctx=None`, then the global context is used.

```pycon
>>> x  = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> is_fp(x)
True
>>> x.ebits()
8
>>> x.sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
>>> word = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x2 = FP('x', word)
>>> eq(x, x2)
True
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FPs
documentation:
Return an array of floating-point constants.

```pycon
>>> x, y, z = FPs('x y z', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> x.sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x.sbits()
24
>>> x.ebits()
8
>>> fpMul(RNE(), fpAdd(RNE(), x, y), z)
(x + y) * z
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpAbs
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point absolute value expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FPVal(1.0, s)
>>> fpAbs(x)
fpAbs(1)
>>> y = FPVal(-20.0, s)
>>> y
-1.25*(2**4)
>>> fpAbs(y)
fpAbs(-1.25*(2**4))
>>> fpAbs(-1.25*(2**4))
fpAbs(-1.25*(2**4))
>>> fpAbs(x).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpNeg
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point addition expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> fpNeg(x)
-x
>>> fpNeg(x).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpAdd
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point addition expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpAdd(rm, x, y)
x + y
>>> fpAdd(RTZ(), x, y) # default rounding mode is RTZ
fpAdd(RTZ(), x, y)
>>> fpAdd(rm, x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpSub
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point subtraction expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpSub(rm, x, y)
x - y
>>> fpSub(rm, x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpMul
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point multiplication expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpMul(rm, x, y)
x * y
>>> fpMul(rm, x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpDiv
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point division expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpDiv(rm, x, y)
x / y
>>> fpDiv(rm, x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpRem
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point remainder expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpRem(x, y)
fpRem(x, y)
>>> fpRem(x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpMin
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point minimum expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpMin(x, y)
fpMin(x, y)
>>> fpMin(x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpMax
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point maximum expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> rm = RNE()
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpMax(x, y)
fpMax(x, y)
>>> fpMax(x, y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpFMA
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point fused multiply-add expression.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpSqrt
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point square root expression.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpRoundToIntegral

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpIsNaN
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point isNaN expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> y = FP('y', s)
>>> fpIsNaN(x)
fpIsNaN(x)
```

documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point isInfinite expression.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x = FP('x', s)
>>> fpIsInf(x)
fpIsInf(x)
```

documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point isZero expression.

documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point isNormal expression.

documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point isSubnormal expression.

documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point isNegative expression.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpLT
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `other < self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpLT(x, y)
x < y
>>> (x < y).sexpr()
'(fp.lt x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpLEQ
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `other <= self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpLEQ(x, y)
x <= y
>>> (x <= y).sexpr()
'(fp.leq x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpGT
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `other > self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpGT(x, y)
x > y
>>> (x > y).sexpr()
'(fp.gt x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpGEQ
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `other >= self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpGEQ(x, y)
x >= y
>>> (x >= y).sexpr()
'(fp.geq x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpEQ
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `fpEQ(other, self)`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpEQ(x, y)
fpEQ(x, y)
>>> fpEQ(x, y).sexpr()
'(fp.eq x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpNEQ
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point expression `Not(fpEQ(other, self))`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = FPs('x y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> fpNEQ(x, y)
Not(fpEQ(x, y))
>>> (x != y).sexpr()
'(distinct x y)'
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpFP
documentation:
Create the Z3 floating-point value `fpFP(sgn, sig, exp)` from the three bit-vectors sgn, sig, and exp.

```pycon
>>> s = FPSort(8, 24)
>>> x = fpFP(BitVecVal(1, 1), BitVecVal(2**7-1, 8), BitVecVal(2**22, 23))
>>> print(x)
fpFP(1, 127, 4194304)
>>> xv = FPVal(-1.5, s)
>>> print(xv)
-1.5
>>> slvr = Solver()
>>> slvr.add(fpEQ(x, xv))
>>> slvr.check()
sat
>>> xv = FPVal(+1.5, s)
>>> print(xv)
1.5
>>> slvr = Solver()
>>> slvr.add(fpEQ(x, xv))
>>> slvr.check()
unsat
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression from other term sorts
to floating-point.

From a bit-vector term in IEEE 754-2008 format:
\>>> x = FPVal(1.0, Float32())
\>>> x_bv = fpToIEEEBV(x)
\>>> simplify(fpToFP(x_bv, Float32()))
1

From a floating-point term with different precision:
\>>> x = FPVal(1.0, Float32())
\>>> x_db = fpToFP(RNE(), x, Float64())
\>>> x_db.sort()
FPSort(11, 53)

From a real term:
\>>> x_r = RealVal(1.5)
\>>> simplify(fpToFP(RNE(), x_r, Float32()))
1.5

From a signed bit-vector term:
\>>> x_signed = BitVecVal(-5, BitVecSort(32))
\>>> simplify(fpToFP(RNE(), x_signed, Float32()))
-1.25\*(2\*\*2)

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpBVToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression that represents the
conversion from a bit-vector term to a floating-point term.

```pycon
>>> x_bv = BitVecVal(0x3F800000, 32)
>>> x_fp = fpBVToFP(x_bv, Float32())
>>> x_fp
fpToFP(1065353216)
>>> simplify(x_fp)
1
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpFPToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression that represents the
conversion from a floating-point term to a floating-point term of different precision.

```pycon
>>> x_sgl = FPVal(1.0, Float32())
>>> x_dbl = fpFPToFP(RNE(), x_sgl, Float64())
>>> x_dbl
fpToFP(RNE(), 1)
>>> simplify(x_dbl)
1
>>> x_dbl.sort()
FPSort(11, 53)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpRealToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression that represents the
conversion from a real term to a floating-point term.

```pycon
>>> x_r = RealVal(1.5)
>>> x_fp = fpRealToFP(RNE(), x_r, Float32())
>>> x_fp
fpToFP(RNE(), 3/2)
>>> simplify(x_fp)
1.5
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpSignedToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression that represents the
conversion from a signed bit-vector term (encoding an integer) to a floating-point term.

```pycon
>>> x_signed = BitVecVal(-5, BitVecSort(32))
>>> x_fp = fpSignedToFP(RNE(), x_signed, Float32())
>>> x_fp
fpToFP(RNE(), 4294967291)
>>> simplify(x_fp)
-1.25*(2**2)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpUnsignedToFP
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression that represents the
conversion from an unsigned bit-vector term (encoding an integer) to a floating-point term.

```pycon
>>> x_signed = BitVecVal(-5, BitVecSort(32))
>>> x_fp = fpUnsignedToFP(RNE(), x_signed, Float32())
>>> x_fp
fpToFPUnsigned(RNE(), 4294967291)
>>> simplify(x_fp)
1*(2**32)
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToFPUnsigned
documentation: Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression, from unsigned bit-vector to floating-point expression.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToSBV
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression, from floating-point expression to signed bit-vector.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = fpToSBV(RTZ(), x, BitVecSort(32))
>>> print(is_fp(x))
True
>>> print(is_bv(y))
True
>>> print(is_fp(y))
False
>>> print(is_bv(x))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToUBV
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression, from floating-point expression to unsigned bit-vector.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = fpToUBV(RTZ(), x, BitVecSort(32))
>>> print(is_fp(x))
True
>>> print(is_bv(y))
True
>>> print(is_fp(y))
False
>>> print(is_bv(x))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToReal
documentation:
Create a Z3 floating-point conversion expression, from floating-point expression to real.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = fpToReal(x)
>>> print(is_fp(x))
True
>>> print(is_real(y))
True
>>> print(is_fp(y))
False
>>> print(is_real(x))
False
```

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.fpToIEEEBV
documentation:
rief Conversion of a floating-point term into a bit-vector term in IEEE 754-2008 format.

The size of the resulting bit-vector is automatically determined.

Note that IEEE 754-2008 allows multiple different representations of NaN. This conversion
knows only one NaN and it will always produce the same bit-vector representation of
that NaN.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = fpToIEEEBV(x)
>>> print(is_fp(x))
True
>>> print(is_bv(y))
True
>>> print(is_fp(y))
False
>>> print(is_bv(x))
False
```

documentation:
Create the empty sequence of the given sort

\>>> print(e.eq(e2))
True

\>>> e1 = Full(ReSort(StringSort()))
\>>> print(e1)
Full(ReSort(String))

Check if 'a' is a prefix of 'b'

\>>> x, y, z = Strings('x y z')

The regular expression that accepts sequence 's'

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.InRe
documentation:
Create regular expression membership test
\>>> re = Union(Re("a"),Re("b"))
\>>> print (simplify(InRe("a", re)))
True
\>>> print (simplify(InRe("b", re)))
True
\>>> print (simplify(InRe("c", re)))
False

documentation:
Create union of regular expressions.
\>>> re = Union(Re("a"), Re("b"), Re("c"))
\>>> print (simplify(InRe("d", re)))

documentation:
Create intersection of regular expressions.
