functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Plus
documentation:
Create the regular expression accepting one or more repetitions of argument.
\>>> re = Plus(Re("a"))
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("aa", re)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("ab", re)))
False
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("", re)))
False

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Option
documentation:
Create the regular expression that optionally accepts the argument.
\>>> re = Option(Re("a"))
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("a", re)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("", re)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("aa", re)))
False

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Complement
documentation: Create the complement regular expression.

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Star
documentation:
Create the regular expression accepting zero or more repetitions of argument.
\>>> re = Star(Re("a"))
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("aa", re)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("ab", re)))
False
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("", re)))
True

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Loop
documentation:
Create the regular expression accepting between a lower and upper bound repetitions
\>>> re = Loop(Re("a"), 1, 3)
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("aa", re)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("aaaa", re)))
False
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("", re)))
False

functions.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Range
documentation:
Create the range regular expression over two sequences of length 1
\>>> range = Range("a","z")
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("b", range)))
True
\>>> print(simplify(InRe("bb", range)))
False

Create a function that gets tracked by user propagator.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Context

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Context.ref
documentation: Return a reference to the actual C pointer to the Z3 context.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Context.interrupt
documentation:
Interrupt a solver performing a satisfiability test, a tactic processing a goal, or simplify functions.

This method can be invoked from a thread different from the one executing the
interruptible procedure.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Context.param_descrs
documentation: Return the global parameter description set.

A Context manages all other Z3 objects, global configuration options, etc.

Z3Py uses a default global context. For most applications this is sufficient.
An application may use multiple Z3 contexts. Objects created in one context
cannot be used in another one. However, several objects may be "translated" from
one context to another. It is not safe to access Z3 objects from multiple threads.
The only exception is the method `interrupt()` that can be used to interrupt() a long
computation.
The initialization method receives global configuration options for the new context.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Z3PPObject
documentation: Superclass for all Z3 objects that have support for pretty printing.
ASTs base class
Mark objects that use pretty printer

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.sexpr
documentation:
Return a string representing the AST node in s-expression notation.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> ((x + 1)*x).sexpr()
'(* (+ x 1) x)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.as_ast
documentation: Return a pointer to the corresponding C Z3_ast object.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.get_id
documentation: Return unique identifier for object. It can be used for hash-tables and maps.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.ctx_ref
documentation: Return a reference to the C context where this AST node is stored.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.eq
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` and `other` are structurally identical.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> n1 = x + 1
>>> n2 = 1 + x
>>> n1.eq(n2)
False
>>> n1 = simplify(n1)
>>> n2 = simplify(n2)
>>> n1.eq(n2)
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.translate
documentation:
Translate `self` to the context `target`. That is, return a copy of `self` in the context `target`.

```pycon
>>> c1 = Context()
>>> c2 = Context()
>>> x  = Int('x', c1)
>>> y  = Int('y', c2)
>>> # Nodes in different contexts can't be mixed.
>>> # However, we can translate nodes from one context to another.
>>> x.translate(c2) + y
x + y
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstRef.hash
documentation:
Return a hashcode for the `self`.

```pycon
>>> n1 = simplify(Int('x') + 1)
>>> n2 = simplify(2 + Int('x') - 1)
>>> n1.hash() == n2.hash()
True
```
AST are Direct Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) used to represent sorts, declarations and expressions.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.kind
documentation:
Return the Z3 internal kind of a sort.
This method can be used to test if `self` is one of the Z3 builtin sorts.

```pycon
>>> b = BoolSort()
>>> b.kind() == Z3_BOOL_SORT
True
>>> b.kind() == Z3_INT_SORT
False
>>> A = ArraySort(IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> A.kind() == Z3_ARRAY_SORT
True
>>> A.kind() == Z3_INT_SORT
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.subsort
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is a subsort of `other`.

```pycon
>>> IntSort().subsort(RealSort())
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.cast
documentation:
Try to cast `val` as an element of sort `self`.

This method is used in Z3Py to convert Python objects such as integers,
floats, longs and strings into Z3 expressions.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> RealSort().cast(x)
ToReal(x)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.name
documentation:
Return the name (string) of sort `self`.

```pycon
>>> BoolSort().name()
'Bool'
>>> ArraySort(IntSort(), IntSort()).name()
'Array'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.__eq__
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` and `other` are the same Z3 sort.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> p.sort() == BoolSort()
True
>>> p.sort() == IntSort()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.__ne__
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` and `other` are not the same Z3 sort.

```pycon
>>> p = Bool('p')
>>> p.sort() != BoolSort()
False
>>> p.sort() != IntSort()
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.SortRef.__hash__
documentation: Hash code.
A Sort is essentially a type. Every Z3 expression has a sort. A sort is an AST node.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.TypeVarRef
documentation: Type variable reference

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.name
documentation:
Return the name of the function declaration `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> f.name()
'f'
>>> isinstance(f.name(), str)
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.arity
documentation:
Return the number of arguments of a function declaration.
If `self` is a constant, then `self.arity()` is 0.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), RealSort(), BoolSort())
>>> f.arity()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.domain
documentation:
Return the sort of the argument `i` of a function declaration.
This method assumes that `0 <= i < self.arity()`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), RealSort(), BoolSort())
>>> f.domain(0)
Int
>>> f.domain(1)
Real
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.range
documentation:
Return the sort of the range of a function declaration.
For constants, this is the sort of the constant.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), RealSort(), BoolSort())
>>> f.range()
Bool
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.kind
documentation:
Return the internal kind of a function declaration.
It can be used to identify Z3 built-in functions such as addition, multiplication, etc.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> d = (x + 1).decl()
>>> d.kind() == Z3_OP_ADD
True
>>> d.kind() == Z3_OP_MUL
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncDeclRef.__call__
documentation:
Create a Z3 application expression using the function `self`, and the given arguments.

The arguments must be Z3 expressions. This method assumes that
the sorts of the elements in `args` match the sorts of the
domain. Limited coercion is supported.  For example, if
args\[0\] is a Python integer, and the function expects a Z3
integer, then the argument is automatically converted into a
Z3 integer.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), RealSort(), BoolSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> f(x, y)
f(x, y)
>>> f(x, x)
f(x, ToReal(x))
```

Function declaration. Every constant and function have an associated declaration.

The declaration assigns a name, a sort (i.e., type), and for function
the sort (i.e., type) of each of its arguments. Note that, in Z3,
a constant is a function with 0 arguments.
Function Declarations

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.sort
documentation:
Return the sort of expression `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> (x + 1).sort()
Int
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> (x + y).sort()
Real
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.sort_kind
documentation:
Shorthand for `self.sort().kind()`.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a.sort_kind() == Z3_ARRAY_SORT
True
>>> a.sort_kind() == Z3_INT_SORT
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.__eq__
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression that represents the constraint `self == other`.

If `other` is `None`, then this method simply returns `False`.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> b = Int('b')
>>> a == b
a == b
>>> a is None
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.__hash__
documentation: Hash code.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.__ne__
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression that represents the constraint `self != other`.

If `other` is `None`, then this method simply returns `True`.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> b = Int('b')
>>> a != b
a != b
>>> a is not None
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.decl
documentation:
Return the Z3 function declaration associated with a Z3 application.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> t = f(a)
>>> eq(t.decl(), f)
True
>>> (a + 1).decl()
+
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.num_args
documentation:
Return the number of arguments of a Z3 application.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> b = Int('b')
>>> (a + b).num_args()
2
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> t = f(a, b, 0)
>>> t.num_args()
3
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.arg
documentation:
Return argument `idx` of the application `self`.

This method assumes that `self` is a function application with at least `idx+1` arguments.

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> b = Int('b')
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> t = f(a, b, 0)
>>> t.arg(0)
a
>>> t.arg(1)
b
>>> t.arg(2)
0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ExprRef.children
documentation:
Return a list containing the children of the given expression

```pycon
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> b = Int('b')
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> t = f(a, b, 0)
>>> t.children()
[a, b, 0]
```

Constraints, formulas and terms are expressions in Z3.

Expressions are ASTs. Every expression has a sort.
There are three main kinds of expressions:
function applications, quantifiers and bounded variables.
A constant is a function application with 0 arguments.
For quantifier free problems, all expressions are
function applications.
Expressions

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolSortRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolSortRef.cast
documentation:
Try to cast `val` as a Boolean.

```pycon
>>> x = BoolSort().cast(True)
>>> x
True
>>> is_expr(x)
True
>>> is_expr(True)
False
>>> x.sort()
Bool
```
Boolean sort.
Booleans

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BoolRef.__mul__
documentation: Create the Z3 expression `self * other`.
All Boolean expressions are instances of this class.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.PatternRef
documentation: Patterns are hints for quantifier instantiation.
Patterns

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.sort
documentation: Return the Boolean sort or sort of Lambda.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.is_forall
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is a universal quantifier.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.is_forall()
True
>>> q = Exists(x, f(x) != 0)
>>> q.is_forall()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.is_exists
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is an existential quantifier.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.is_exists()
False
>>> q = Exists(x, f(x) != 0)
>>> q.is_exists()
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.is_lambda
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is a lambda expression.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = Lambda(x, f(x))
>>> q.is_lambda()
True
>>> q = Exists(x, f(x) != 0)
>>> q.is_lambda()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.__getitem__
documentation: Return the Z3 expression `self[arg]`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.weight
documentation:
Return the weight annotation of `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.weight()
1
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0, weight=10)
>>> q.weight()
10
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.skolem_id
documentation: Return the skolem id of `self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.qid
documentation: Return the quantifier id of `self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.num_patterns
documentation:
Return the number of patterns (i.e., quantifier instantiation hints) in `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> g = Function('g', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) != g(x), patterns = [ f(x), g(x) ])
>>> q.num_patterns()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.pattern
documentation:
Return a pattern (i.e., quantifier instantiation hints) in `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> g = Function('g', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) != g(x), patterns = [ f(x), g(x) ])
>>> q.num_patterns()
2
>>> q.pattern(0)
f(Var(0))
>>> q.pattern(1)
g(Var(0))
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.num_no_patterns
documentation: Return the number of no-patterns.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.no_pattern
documentation: Return a no-pattern.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.body
documentation:
Return the expression being quantified.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.body()
f(Var(0)) == 0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.num_vars
documentation:
Return the number of variables bounded by this quantifier.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> q = ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> q.num_vars()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.var_name
documentation:
Return a string representing a name used when displaying the quantifier.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> q = ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> q.var_name(0)
'x'
>>> q.var_name(1)
'y'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.var_sort
documentation:
Return the sort of a bound variable.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), RealSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> q = ForAll([x, y], f(x, y) >= x)
>>> q.var_sort(0)
Int
>>> q.var_sort(1)
Real
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.QuantifierRef.children
documentation:
Return a list containing a single element self.body()

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> q = ForAll(x, f(x) == 0)
>>> q.children()
[f(Var(0)) == 0]
```
Universally and Existentially quantified formulas.
Quantifiers

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithSortRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithSortRef.is_real
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is of the sort Real.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x.is_real()
True
>>> (x + 1).is_real()
True
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> x.is_real()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithSortRef.is_int
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is of the sort Integer.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> x.is_int()
True
>>> (x + 1).is_int()
True
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x.is_int()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithSortRef.subsort
documentation: Return `True` if `self` is a subsort of `other`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithSortRef.cast
documentation:
Try to cast `val` as an Integer or Real.

```pycon
>>> IntSort().cast(10)
10
>>> is_int(IntSort().cast(10))
True
>>> is_int(10)
False
>>> RealSort().cast(10)
10
>>> is_real(RealSort().cast(10))
True
```
Real and Integer sorts.
Arithmetic

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.sort
documentation:
Return the sort (type) of the arithmetical expression `self`.

```pycon
>>> Int('x').sort()
Int
>>> (Real('x') + 1).sort()
Real
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.is_int
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is an integer expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> x.is_int()
True
>>> (x + 1).is_int()
True
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> (x + y).is_int()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.is_real
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` is an real expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x.is_real()
True
>>> (x + 1).is_real()
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__add__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self + other`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> x + y
x + y
>>> (x + y).sort()
Int
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__radd__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other + self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> 10 + x
10 + x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__mul__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self * other`.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x * y
x*y
>>> (x * y).sort()
Real
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rmul__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other * self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> 10 * x
10*x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__sub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self - other`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> x - y
x - y
>>> (x - y).sort()
Int
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rsub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other - self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> 10 - x
10 - x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__pow__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self**other` (\*\* is the power operator).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> x**3
x**3
>>> (x**3).sort()
Real
>>> simplify(IntVal(2)**8)
256
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rpow__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other**self` (\*\* is the power operator).

```pycon
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> 2**x
2**x
>>> (2**x).sort()
Real
>>> simplify(2**IntVal(8))
256
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__div__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other/self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> x/y
x/y
>>> (x/y).sort()
Int
>>> (x/y).sexpr()
'(div x y)'
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x/y
x/y
>>> (x/y).sort()
Real
>>> (x/y).sexpr()
'(/ x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__truediv__
documentation: Create the Z3 expression `other/self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rdiv__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other/self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> 10/x
10/x
>>> (10/x).sexpr()
'(div 10 x)'
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> 10/x
10/x
>>> (10/x).sexpr()
'(/ 10.0 x)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rtruediv__
documentation: Create the Z3 expression `other/self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__mod__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other%self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> x % y
x%y
>>> simplify(IntVal(10) % IntVal(3))
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__rmod__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other%self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> 10 % x
10%x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__neg__
documentation:
Return an expression representing `-self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> -x
-x
>>> simplify(-(-x))
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__pos__
documentation:
Return `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> +x
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__le__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other <= self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> x <= y
x <= y
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x <= y
ToReal(x) <= y
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__lt__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other < self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> x < y
x < y
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x < y
ToReal(x) < y
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__gt__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other > self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> x > y
x > y
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x > y
ToReal(x) > y
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArithRef.__ge__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other >= self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> x >= y
x >= y
>>> y = Real('y')
>>> x >= y
ToReal(x) >= y
```
Integer and Real expressions.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntNumRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntNumRef.as_long
documentation:
Return a Z3 integer numeral as a Python long (bignum) numeral.

```pycon
>>> v = IntVal(1)
>>> v + 1
1 + 1
>>> v.as_long() + 1
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntNumRef.as_string
documentation:
Return a Z3 integer numeral as a Python string.
\>>> v = IntVal(100)
\>>> v.as_string()
'100'

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.IntNumRef.as_binary_string
documentation:
Return a Z3 integer numeral as a Python binary string.
\>>> v = IntVal(10)
\>>> v.as_binary_string()
'1010'
Integer values.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.numerator
documentation:
Return the numerator of a Z3 rational numeral.

```pycon
>>> is_rational_value(RealVal("3/5"))
True
>>> n = RealVal("3/5")
>>> n.numerator()
3
>>> is_rational_value(Q(3,5))
True
>>> Q(3,5).numerator()
3
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.denominator
documentation:
Return the denominator of a Z3 rational numeral.

```pycon
>>> is_rational_value(Q(3,5))
True
>>> n = Q(3,5)
>>> n.denominator()
5
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.numerator_as_long
documentation:
Return the numerator as a Python long.

```pycon
>>> v = RealVal(10000000000)
>>> v
10000000000
>>> v + 1
10000000000 + 1
>>> v.numerator_as_long() + 1 == 10000000001
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.denominator_as_long
documentation:
Return the denominator as a Python long.

```pycon
>>> v = RealVal("1/3")
>>> v
1/3
>>> v.denominator_as_long()
3
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.as_decimal
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational value as a string in decimal notation using at most `prec` decimal places.

```pycon
>>> v = RealVal("1/5")
>>> v.as_decimal(3)
'0.2'
>>> v = RealVal("1/3")
>>> v.as_decimal(3)
'0.333?'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.as_string
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational numeral as a Python string.

```pycon
>>> v = Q(3,6)
>>> v.as_string()
'1/2'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.RatNumRef.as_fraction
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational as a Python Fraction object.

```pycon
>>> v = RealVal("1/5")
>>> v.as_fraction()
Fraction(1, 5)
```
Rational values.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AlgebraicNumRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AlgebraicNumRef.approx
documentation:
Return a Z3 rational number that approximates the algebraic number `self`.
The result `r` is such that {{ r - self }} \<= 1/10^precision

```pycon
>>> x = simplify(Sqrt(2))
>>> x.approx(20)
6838717160008073720548335/4835703278458516698824704
>>> x.approx(5)
2965821/2097152
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AlgebraicNumRef.as_decimal
documentation:
Return a string representation of the algebraic number `self` in decimal notation
using `prec` decimal places.

```pycon
>>> x = simplify(Sqrt(2))
>>> x.as_decimal(10)
'1.4142135623?'
>>> x.as_decimal(20)
'1.41421356237309504880?'
```
Algebraic irrational values.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecSortRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecSortRef.size
documentation:
Return the size (number of bits) of the bit-vector sort `self`.

```pycon
>>> b = BitVecSort(32)
>>> b.size()
32
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecSortRef.cast
documentation:
Try to cast `val` as a Bit-Vector.

```pycon
>>> b = BitVecSort(32)
>>> b.cast(10)
10
>>> b.cast(10).sexpr()
'#x0000000a'
```

Bit-Vectors

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.sort
documentation:
Return the sort of the bit-vector expression `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.size
documentation:
Return the number of bits of the bit-vector expression `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> (x + 1).size()

>>> Concat(x, x).size()

```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__add__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self + other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x + y
x + y
>>> (x + y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__radd__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other + self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 + x
10 + x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__mul__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self * other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x * y
x*y
>>> (x * y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rmul__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other * self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 * x
10*x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__sub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self - other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x - y
x - y
>>> (x - y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rsub__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `other - self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 - x
10 - x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__or__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-or `self | other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x | y
x | y
>>> (x | y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__ror__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-or `other | self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 | x
10 | x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__and__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-and `self & other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x & y
x & y
>>> (x & y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rand__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-or `other & self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 & x
10 & x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__xor__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-xor `self ^ other`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x ^ y
x ^ y
>>> (x ^ y).sort()
BitVec(32)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rxor__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-xor `other ^ self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 ^ x
10 ^ x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__pos__
documentation:
Return `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> +x
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__neg__
documentation:
Return an expression representing `-self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> -x
-x
>>> simplify(-(-x))
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__invert__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression bitwise-not `~self`.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> ~x
~x
>>> simplify(~(~x))
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__div__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) division `self / other`.

Use the function UDiv() for unsigned division.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x / y
x/y
>>> (x / y).sort()
BitVec(32)
>>> (x / y).sexpr()
'(bvsdiv x y)'
>>> UDiv(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvudiv x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__truediv__
documentation: Create the Z3 expression (signed) division `self / other`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rdiv__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) division `other / self`.

Use the function UDiv() for unsigned division.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 / x
10/x
>>> (10 / x).sexpr()
'(bvsdiv #x0000000a x)'
>>> UDiv(10, x).sexpr()
'(bvudiv #x0000000a x)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rtruediv__
documentation: Create the Z3 expression (signed) division `other / self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__mod__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) mod `self % other`.

Use the function URem() for unsigned remainder, and SRem() for signed remainder.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> y = BitVec('y', 32)
>>> x % y
x%y
>>> (x % y).sort()
BitVec(32)
>>> (x % y).sexpr()
'(bvsmod x y)'
>>> URem(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvurem x y)'
>>> SRem(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvsrem x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rmod__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) mod `other % self`.

Use the function URem() for unsigned remainder, and SRem() for signed remainder.

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 % x
10%x
>>> (10 % x).sexpr()
'(bvsmod #x0000000a x)'
>>> URem(10, x).sexpr()
'(bvurem #x0000000a x)'
>>> SRem(10, x).sexpr()
'(bvsrem #x0000000a x)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__le__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) `other <= self`.

Use the function ULE() for unsigned less than or equal to.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x <= y
x <= y
>>> (x <= y).sexpr()
'(bvsle x y)'
>>> ULE(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvule x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__lt__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) `other < self`.

Use the function ULT() for unsigned less than.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x < y
x < y
>>> (x < y).sexpr()
'(bvslt x y)'
>>> ULT(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvult x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__gt__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) `other > self`.

Use the function UGT() for unsigned greater than.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x > y
x > y
>>> (x > y).sexpr()
'(bvsgt x y)'
>>> UGT(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvugt x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__ge__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (signed) `other >= self`.

Use the function UGE() for unsigned greater than or equal to.

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x >= y
x >= y
>>> (x >= y).sexpr()
'(bvsge x y)'
>>> UGE(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvuge x y)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rshift__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (arithmetical) right shift `self >> other`

Use the function LShR() for the right logical shift

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x >> y
x >> y
>>> (x >> y).sexpr()
'(bvashr x y)'
>>> LShR(x, y).sexpr()
'(bvlshr x y)'
>>> BitVecVal(4, 3)
4
>>> BitVecVal(4, 3).as_signed_long()
-4
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(4, 3) >> 1).as_signed_long()
-2
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(4, 3) >> 1)
6
>>> simplify(LShR(BitVecVal(4, 3), 1))
2
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(2, 3) >> 1)
1
>>> simplify(LShR(BitVecVal(2, 3), 1))
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__lshift__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression left shift `self << other`

```pycon
>>> x, y = BitVecs('x y', 32)
>>> x << y
x << y
>>> (x << y).sexpr()
'(bvshl x y)'
>>> simplify(BitVecVal(2, 3) << 1)
4
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rrshift__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression (arithmetical) right shift `other` >> `self`.

Use the function LShR() for the right logical shift

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 >> x
10 >> x
>>> (10 >> x).sexpr()
'(bvashr #x0000000a x)'
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecRef.__rlshift__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression left shift `other << self`.

Use the function LShR() for the right logical shift

```pycon
>>> x = BitVec('x', 32)
>>> 10 << x
10 << x
>>> (10 << x).sexpr()
'(bvshl #x0000000a x)'
```
Bit-vector expressions.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecNumRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecNumRef.as_long
documentation:
Return a Z3 bit-vector numeral as a Python long (bignum) numeral.

```pycon
>>> v = BitVecVal(0xbadc0de, 32)
>>> v
195936478
>>> print("0x%.8x" % v.as_long())
0x0badc0de
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.BitVecNumRef.as_signed_long
documentation:
Return a Z3 bit-vector numeral as a Python long (bignum) numeral.
The most significant bit is assumed to be the sign.

```pycon
>>> BitVecVal(4, 3).as_signed_long()
-4
>>> BitVecVal(7, 3).as_signed_long()
-1
>>> BitVecVal(3, 3).as_signed_long()
3
>>> BitVecVal(2**32 - 1, 32).as_signed_long()
-1
>>> BitVecVal(2**64 - 1, 64).as_signed_long()
-1
```
Bit-vector values.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ArrayRef.__getitem__
documentation:
Return the Z3 expression `self[arg]`.

```pycon
>>> a = Array('a', IntSort(), BoolSort())
>>> i = Int('i')
>>> a[i]
a[i]
>>> a[i].sexpr()
'(select a i)'
```

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Datatype

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Datatype.declare
documentation:
Declare constructor named `name` with the given accessors `args`.
Each accessor is a pair `(name, sort)`, where `name` is a string and `sort` a Z3 sort
or a reference to the datatypes being declared.

In the following example `List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))`
declares the constructor named `cons` that builds a new List using an integer and a List.
It also declares the accessors `car` and `cdr`. The accessor `car` extracts the integer
of a `cons` cell, and `cdr` the list of a `cons` cell. After all constructors were declared,
we use the method create() to create the actual datatype in Z3.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Datatype.create
documentation:
Create a Z3 datatype based on the constructors declared using the method `declare()`.

The function `CreateDatatypes()` must be used to define mutually recursive datatypes.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> List.nil
nil
>>> List.cons(10, List.nil)
cons(10, nil)
```

Helper class for declaring Z3 datatypes.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> # List is now a Z3 declaration
>>> List.nil
nil
>>> List.cons(10, List.nil)
cons(10, nil)
>>> List.cons(10, List.nil).sort()
List
>>> cons = List.cons
>>> nil  = List.nil
>>> car  = List.car
>>> cdr  = List.cdr
>>> n = cons(1, cons(0, nil))
>>> n
cons(1, cons(0, nil))
>>> simplify(cdr(n))
cons(0, nil)
>>> simplify(car(n))
1
```

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ScopedConstructor
documentation: Auxiliary object used to create Z3 datatypes.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ScopedConstructorList
documentation: Auxiliary object used to create Z3 datatypes.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSortRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSortRef.num_constructors
documentation:
Return the number of constructors in the given Z3 datatype.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> # List is now a Z3 declaration
>>> List.num_constructors()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSortRef.constructor
documentation:
Return a constructor of the datatype `self`.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> # List is now a Z3 declaration
>>> List.num_constructors()
2
>>> List.constructor(0)
cons
>>> List.constructor(1)
nil
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSortRef.recognizer
documentation:
In Z3, each constructor has an associated recognizer predicate.

If the constructor is named `name`, then the recognizer `is_name`.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> # List is now a Z3 declaration
>>> List.num_constructors()
2
>>> List.recognizer(0)
is(cons)
>>> List.recognizer(1)
is(nil)
>>> simplify(List.is_nil(List.cons(10, List.nil)))
False
>>> simplify(List.is_cons(List.cons(10, List.nil)))
True
>>> l = Const('l', List)
>>> simplify(List.is_cons(l))
is(cons, l)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.DatatypeSortRef.accessor
documentation:
In Z3, each constructor has 0 or more accessor.
The number of accessors is equal to the arity of the constructor.

```pycon
>>> List = Datatype('List')
>>> List.declare('cons', ('car', IntSort()), ('cdr', List))
>>> List.declare('nil')
>>> List = List.create()
>>> List.num_constructors()
2
>>> List.constructor(0)
cons
>>> num_accs = List.constructor(0).arity()
>>> num_accs
2
>>> List.accessor(0, 0)
car
>>> List.accessor(0, 1)
cdr
>>> List.constructor(1)
nil
>>> num_accs = List.constructor(1).arity()
>>> num_accs
0
```
Datatype sorts.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ParamDescrsRef.size
documentation: Return the size of in the parameter description `self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ParamDescrsRef.__len__
documentation: Return the size of in the parameter description `self`.

documentation: Return the i-th parameter name in the parameter description `self`.

documentation: Return the kind of the parameter named `n`.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.depth
documentation:
Return the depth of the goal `self`.
The depth corresponds to the number of tactics applied to `self`.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x == 0, y >= x + 1)
>>> g.depth()
0
>>> r = Then('simplify', 'solve-eqs')(g)
>>> # r has 1 subgoal
>>> len(r)
1
>>> r[0].depth()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.inconsistent
documentation:
Return `True` if `self` contains the `False` constraints.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.inconsistent()
False
>>> g.add(x == 0, x == 1)
>>> g
[x == 0, x == 1]
>>> g.inconsistent()
False
>>> g2 = Tactic('propagate-values')(g)[0]
>>> g2.inconsistent()
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.prec
documentation:
Return the precision (under-approximation, over-approximation, or precise) of the goal `self`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.prec() == Z3_GOAL_PRECISE
True
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g.add(x == y + 1)
>>> g.prec() == Z3_GOAL_PRECISE
True
>>> t  = With(Tactic('add-bounds'), add_bound_lower=0, add_bound_upper=10)
>>> g2 = t(g)[0]
>>> g2
[x == y + 1, x <= 10, x >= 0, y <= 10, y >= 0]
>>> g2.prec() == Z3_GOAL_PRECISE
False
>>> g2.prec() == Z3_GOAL_UNDER
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.precision
documentation:
Alias for `prec()`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.precision() == Z3_GOAL_PRECISE
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.size
documentation:
Return the number of constraints in the goal `self`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.size()
0
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g.add(x == 0, y > x)
>>> g.size()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.__len__
documentation:
Return the number of constraints in the goal `self`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> len(g)
0
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g.add(x == 0, y > x)
>>> len(g)
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.get
documentation:
Return a constraint in the goal `self`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g.add(x == 0, y > x)
>>> g.get(0)
x == 0
>>> g.get(1)
y > x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.__getitem__
documentation:
Return a constraint in the goal `self`.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> g.add(x == 0, y > x)
>>> g[0]
x == 0
>>> g[1]
y > x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.assert_exprs
documentation:
Assert constraints into the goal.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.assert_exprs(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> g
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.append
documentation:
Add constraints.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.append(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> g
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.insert
documentation:
Add constraints.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.insert(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> g
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.add
documentation:
Add constraints.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> g
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.convert_model
documentation:
Retrieve model from a satisfiable goal
\>>> a, b = Ints('a b')
\>>> g = Goal()
\>>> g.add(Or(a == 0, a == 1), Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b)
\>>> t = Then(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('solve-eqs'))
\>>> r = t(g)
\>>> r\[0\]
\[Or(b == 0, b == 1), Not(0 \<= b)\]
\>>> r\[1\]
\[Or(b == 0, b == 1), Not(1 \<= b)\]
\>>> # Remark: the subgoal r\[0\] is unsatisfiable
\>>> # Creating a solver for solving the second subgoal
\>>> s = Solver()
\>>> s.add(r\[1\])
\>>> s.check()
sat
\>>> s.model()
\[b = 0\]
\>>> # Model s.model() does not assign a value to `a`
\>>> # It is a model for subgoal `r[1]`, but not for goal `g`
\>>> # The method convert_model creates a model for `g` from a model for `r[1]`.
\>>> r\[1\].convert_model(s.model())
\[b = 0, a = 1\]

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.sexpr
documentation: Return a textual representation of the s-expression representing the goal.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.dimacs
documentation: Return a textual representation of the goal in DIMACS format.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.translate
documentation:
Copy goal `self` to context `target`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 10)
>>> g
[x > 10]
>>> c2 = Context()
>>> g2 = g.translate(c2)
>>> g2
[x > 10]
>>> g.ctx == main_ctx()
True
>>> g2.ctx == c2
True
>>> g2.ctx == main_ctx()
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.simplify
documentation:
Return a new simplified goal.

This method is essentially invoking the simplify tactic.

```pycon
>>> g = Goal()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g.add(x + 1 >= 2)
>>> g
[x + 1 >= 2]
>>> g2 = g.simplify()
>>> g2
[x >= 1]
>>> # g was not modified
>>> g
[x + 1 >= 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Goal.as_expr
documentation:
Return goal `self` as a single Z3 expression.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.as_expr()
True
>>> g.add(x > 1)
>>> g.as_expr()
x > 1
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> g.as_expr()
And(x > 1, x < 10)
```

Goal is a collection of constraints we want to find a solution or show to be unsatisfiable (infeasible).

Goals are processed using Tactics. A Tactic transforms a goal into a set of subgoals.
A goal has a solution if one of its subgoals has a solution.
A goal is unsatisfiable if all subgoals are unsatisfiable.
Goals

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.__len__
documentation:
Return the size of the vector `self`.

```pycon
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> len(A)
0
>>> A.push(Int('x'))
>>> A.push(Int('x'))
>>> len(A)
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.__getitem__
documentation:
Return the AST at position `i`.

```pycon
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> A.push(Int('x') + 1)
>>> A.push(Int('y'))
>>> A[0]
x + 1
>>> A[1]
y
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.__setitem__
documentation:
Update AST at position `i`.

```pycon
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> A.push(Int('x') + 1)
>>> A.push(Int('y'))
>>> A[0]
x + 1
>>> A[0] = Int('x')
>>> A[0]
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.push
documentation:
Add `v` in the end of the vector.

```pycon
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> len(A)
0
>>> A.push(Int('x'))
>>> len(A)
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.resize
documentation:
Resize the vector to `sz` elements.

```pycon
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> A.resize(10)
>>> len(A)
10
>>> for i in range(10): A[i] = Int('x')
>>> A[5]
x
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.__contains__
documentation:
Return `True` if the vector contains `item`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> x in A
False
>>> A.push(x)
>>> x in A
True
>>> (x+1) in A
False
>>> A.push(x+1)
>>> (x+1) in A
True
>>> A
[x, x + 1]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.translate
documentation:
Copy vector `self` to context `other_ctx`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> A = AstVector()
>>> A.push(x)
>>> c2 = Context()
>>> B = A.translate(c2)
>>> B
[x]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstVector.sexpr
documentation: Return a textual representation of the s-expression representing the vector.
A collection (vector) of ASTs.
AST Vector

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.__len__
documentation:
Return the size of the map.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> len(M)
0
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x] = IntVal(1)
>>> len(M)
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.__contains__
documentation:
Return `True` if the map contains key `key`.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x] = x + 1
>>> x in M
True
>>> x+1 in M
False
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.__getitem__
documentation:
Retrieve the value associated with key `key`.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x] = x + 1
>>> M[x]
x + 1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.__setitem__
documentation:
Add/Update key `k` with value `v`.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x] = x + 1
>>> len(M)
1
>>> M[x]
x + 1
>>> M[x] = IntVal(1)
>>> M[x]
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.erase
documentation:
Remove the entry associated with key `k`.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x] = x + 1
>>> len(M)
1
>>> M.erase(x)
>>> len(M)
0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.reset
documentation:
Remove all entries from the map.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x]   = x + 1
>>> M[x+x] = IntVal(1)
>>> len(M)
2
>>> M.reset()
>>> len(M)
0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.AstMap.keys
documentation:
Return an AstVector containing all keys in the map.

```pycon
>>> M = AstMap()
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> M[x]   = x + 1
>>> M[x+x] = IntVal(1)
>>> M.keys()
[x, x + x]
```
A mapping from ASTs to ASTs.
AST Map

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncEntry

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncEntry.num_args
documentation:
Return the number of arguments in the given entry.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0, 1) == 10, f(1, 2) == 20, f(1, 0) == 10)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> f_i = m[f]
>>> f_i.num_entries()
1
>>> e = f_i.entry(0)
>>> e.num_args()
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncEntry.arg_value
documentation:
Return the value of argument `idx`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0, 1) == 10, f(1, 2) == 20, f(1, 0) == 10)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> f_i = m[f]
>>> f_i.num_entries()
1
>>> e = f_i.entry(0)
>>> e
[1, 2, 20]
>>> e.num_args()
2
>>> e.arg_value(0)
1
>>> e.arg_value(1)
2
>>> try:
...   e.arg_value(2)
... except IndexError:
...   print("index error")
index error
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncEntry.value
documentation:
Return the value of the function at point `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0, 1) == 10, f(1, 2) == 20, f(1, 0) == 10)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> f_i = m[f]
>>> f_i.num_entries()
1
>>> e = f_i.entry(0)
>>> e
[1, 2, 20]
>>> e.num_args()
2
>>> e.value()
20
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncEntry.as_list
documentation:
Return entry `self` as a Python list.
\>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort(), IntSort())
\>>> s = Solver()
\>>> s.add(f(0, 1) == 10, f(1, 2) == 20, f(1, 0) == 10)
\>>> s.check()
sat
\>>> m = s.model()
\>>> f_i = m\[f\]
\>>> f_i.num_entries()
1
\>>> e = f_i.entry(0)
\>>> e.as_list()
\[1, 2, 20\]
Store the value of the interpretation of a function in a particular point.
Model

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.else_value
documentation:
Return the `else` value for a function interpretation.
Return None if Z3 did not specify the `else` value for
this object.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0) == 1, f(1) == 1, f(2) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[f]
[2 -> 0, else -> 1]
>>> m[f].else_value()
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.num_entries
documentation:
Return the number of entries/points in the function interpretation `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0) == 1, f(1) == 1, f(2) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[f]
[2 -> 0, else -> 1]
>>> m[f].num_entries()
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.arity
documentation:
Return the number of arguments for each entry in the function interpretation `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0) == 1, f(1) == 1, f(2) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[f].arity()
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.entry
documentation:
Return an entry at position `idx < self.num_entries()` in the function interpretation `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(f(0) == 1, f(1) == 1, f(2) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[f]
[2 -> 0, else -> 1]
>>> m[f].num_entries()
1
>>> m[f].entry(0)
[2, 0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.translate
documentation: Copy model 'self' to context 'other_ctx'.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FuncInterp.as_list
documentation:
Return the function interpretation as a Python list.
\>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
\>>> s = Solver()
\>>> s.add(f(0) == 1, f(1) == 1, f(2) == 0)
\>>> s.check()
sat
\>>> m = s.model()
\>>> m\[f\]
\[2 -> 0, else -> 1\]
\>>> m\[f\].as_list()
\[\[2, 0\], 1\]
Stores the interpretation of a function in a Z3 model.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.sexpr
documentation: Return a textual representation of the s-expression representing the model.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.eval
documentation:
Evaluate the expression `t` in the model `self`.
If `model_completion` is enabled, then a default interpretation is automatically added
for symbols that do not have an interpretation in the model `self`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.eval(x + 1)
2
>>> m.eval(x == 1)
True
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> m.eval(y + x)
1 + y
>>> m.eval(y)
y
>>> m.eval(y, model_completion=True)
0
>>> # Now, m contains an interpretation for y
>>> m.eval(y + x)
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.evaluate
documentation:
Alias for `eval`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.evaluate(x + 1)
2
>>> m.evaluate(x == 1)
True
>>> y = Int('y')
>>> m.evaluate(y + x)
1 + y
>>> m.evaluate(y)
y
>>> m.evaluate(y, model_completion=True)
0
>>> # Now, m contains an interpretation for y
>>> m.evaluate(y + x)
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.__len__
documentation:
Return the number of constant and function declarations in the model `self`.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, f(x) != x)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> len(m)
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.get_interp
documentation:
Return the interpretation for a given declaration or constant.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2, f(x) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m[x]
1
>>> m[f]
[else -> 0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.num_sorts
documentation:
Return the number of uninterpreted sorts that contain an interpretation in the model `self`.

```pycon
>>> A = DeclareSort('A')
>>> a, b = Consts('a b', A)
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(a != b)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.num_sorts()
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.get_sort
documentation:
Return the uninterpreted sort at position `idx` \< self.num_sorts().

```pycon
>>> A = DeclareSort('A')
>>> B = DeclareSort('B')
>>> a1, a2 = Consts('a1 a2', A)
>>> b1, b2 = Consts('b1 b2', B)
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(a1 != a2, b1 != b2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.num_sorts()
2
>>> m.get_sort(0)
A
>>> m.get_sort(1)
B
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.sorts
documentation:
Return all uninterpreted sorts that have an interpretation in the model `self`.

```pycon
>>> A = DeclareSort('A')
>>> B = DeclareSort('B')
>>> a1, a2 = Consts('a1 a2', A)
>>> b1, b2 = Consts('b1 b2', B)
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(a1 != a2, b1 != b2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.sorts()
[A, B]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.get_universe
documentation:
Return the interpretation for the uninterpreted sort `s` in the model `self`.

```pycon
>>> A = DeclareSort('A')
>>> a, b = Consts('a b', A)
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(a != b)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> m.get_universe(A)
[A!val!1, A!val!0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.__getitem__
documentation:
If `idx` is an integer, then the declaration at position `idx` in the model `self` is returned.
If `idx` is a declaration, then the actual interpretation is returned.

The elements can be retrieved using position or the actual declaration.

```pycon
>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2, f(x) == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> m = s.model()
>>> len(m)
2
>>> m[0]
x
>>> m[1]
f
>>> m[x]
1
>>> m[f]
[else -> 0]
>>> for d in m: print("%s -> %s" % (d, m[d]))
x -> 1
f -> [else -> 0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.decls
documentation:
Return a list with all symbols that have an interpretation in the model `self`.
\>>> f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())
\>>> x = Int('x')
\>>> s = Solver()
\>>> s.add(x > 0, x \< 2, f(x) == 0)
\>>> s.check()
sat
\>>> m = s.model()
\>>> m.decls()
\[x, f\]

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.update_value
documentation: Update the interpretation of a constant

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ModelRef.translate
documentation: Translate `self` to the context `target`. That is, return a copy of `self` in the context `target`.
Model/Solution of a satisfiability problem (aka system of constraints).

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics.__len__
documentation:
Return the number of statistical counters.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Then('simplify', 'nlsat').solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> st = s.statistics()
>>> len(st)
7
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics.__getitem__
documentation:
Return the value of statistical counter at position `idx`. The result is a pair (key, value).

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Then('simplify', 'nlsat').solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> st = s.statistics()
>>> len(st)
7
>>> st[0]
('nlsat propagations', 2)
>>> st[1]
('nlsat restarts', 1)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics.keys
documentation:
Return the list of statistical counters.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Then('simplify', 'nlsat').solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> st = s.statistics()
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics.get_key_value
documentation:
Return the value of a particular statistical counter.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Then('simplify', 'nlsat').solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> st = s.statistics()
>>> st.get_key_value('nlsat propagations')
2
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Statistics.__getattr__
documentation:
Access the value of statistical using attributes.

Remark: to access a counter containing blank spaces (e.g., 'nlsat propagations'),
we should use '\_' (e.g., 'nlsat_propagations').

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Then('simplify', 'nlsat').solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> st = s.statistics()
>>> st.nlsat_propagations
2
>>> st.nlsat_stages
2
```
Statistics for `Solver.check()`.
Statistics

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.CheckSatResult
documentation:
Represents the result of a satisfiability check: sat, unsat, unknown.

```pycon
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> r = s.check()
>>> isinstance(r, CheckSatResult)
True
```
Solver

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.set
documentation:
Set a configuration option.
The method `help()` return a string containing all available options.

```pycon
>>> s = Solver()
>>> # The option MBQI can be set using three different approaches.
>>> s.set(mbqi=True)
>>> s.set('MBQI', True)
>>> s.set(':mbqi', True)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.push
documentation:
Create a backtracking point.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s
[x > 0]
>>> s.push()
>>> s.add(x < 1)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 1]
>>> s.check()
unsat
>>> s.pop()
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s
[x > 0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.pop
documentation:
Backtrack c num backtracking points.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s
[x > 0]
>>> s.push()
>>> s.add(x < 1)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 1]
>>> s.check()
unsat
>>> s.pop()
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s
[x > 0]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.num_scopes
documentation:
Return the current number of backtracking points.

```pycon
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.num_scopes()
0
>>> s.push()
>>> s.num_scopes()
1
>>> s.push()
>>> s.num_scopes()
2
>>> s.pop()
>>> s.num_scopes()
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.reset
documentation:
Remove all asserted constraints and backtracking points created using `push()`.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0)
>>> s
[x > 0]
>>> s.reset()
>>> s
[]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.assert_exprs
documentation:
Assert constraints into the solver.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.assert_exprs(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.add
documentation:
Assert constraints into the solver.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.append
documentation:
Assert constraints into the solver.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.append(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.insert
documentation:
Assert constraints into the solver.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.insert(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s
[x > 0, x < 2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.assert_and_track
documentation:
Assert constraint `a` and track it in the unsat core using the Boolean constant `p`.

If `p` is a string, it will be automatically converted into a Boolean constant.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> p3 = Bool('p3')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.set(unsat_core=True)
>>> s.assert_and_track(x > 0,  'p1')
>>> s.assert_and_track(x != 1, 'p2')
>>> s.assert_and_track(x < 0,  p3)
>>> print(s.check())
unsat
>>> c = s.unsat_core()
>>> len(c)
2
>>> Bool('p1') in c
True
>>> Bool('p2') in c
False
>>> p3 in c
True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.check
documentation:
Check whether the assertions in the given solver plus the optional assumptions are consistent or not.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> s = Solver()
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.add(x > 0, x < 2)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model().eval(x)
1
>>> s.add(x < 1)
>>> s.check()
unsat
>>> s.reset()
>>> s.add(2**x == 4)
>>> s.check()
unknown
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.model
documentation:
Return a model for the last `check()`.

This function raises an exception if
a model is not available (e.g., last `check()` returned unsat).

```pycon
>>> s = Solver()
>>> a = Int('a')
>>> s.add(a + 2 == 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model()
[a = -2]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.import_model_converter
documentation: Import model converter from other into the current solver

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.interrupt
documentation:
Interrupt the execution of the solver object.
Remarks: This ensures that the interrupt applies only
to the given solver object and it applies only if it is running.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.unsat_core
documentation:
Return a subset (as an AST vector) of the assumptions provided to the last check().

These are the assumptions Z3 used in the unsatisfiability proof.
Assumptions are available in Z3. They are used to extract unsatisfiable cores.
They may be also used to "retract" assumptions. Note that, assumptions are not really
"soft constraints", but they can be used to implement them.

```pycon
>>> p1, p2, p3 = Bools('p1 p2 p3')
>>> x, y       = Ints('x y')
>>> s          = Solver()
>>> s.add(Implies(p1, x > 0))
>>> s.add(Implies(p2, y > x))
>>> s.add(Implies(p2, y < 1))
>>> s.add(Implies(p3, y > -3))
>>> s.check(p1, p2, p3)
unsat
>>> core = s.unsat_core()
>>> len(core)
2
>>> p1 in core
True
>>> p2 in core
True
>>> p3 in core
False
>>> # "Retracting" p2
>>> s.check(p1, p3)
sat
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.consequences
documentation:
Determine fixed values for the variables based on the solver state and assumptions.
\>>> s = Solver()
\>>> a, b, c, d = Bools('a b c d')
\>>> s.add(Implies(a,b), Implies(b, c))
\>>> s.consequences(\[a\],\[b,c,d\])
(sat, \[Implies(a, b), Implies(a, c)\])
\>>> s.consequences(\[Not(c),d\],\[a,b,c,d\])
(sat, \[Implies(d, d), Implies(Not(c), Not(c)), Implies(Not(c), Not(b)), Implies(Not(c), Not(a))\])

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.cube

Get set of cubes
The method takes an optional set of variables that restrict which
variables may be used as a starting point for cubing.
If vars is not None, then the first case split is based on a variable in
this set.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.cube_vars
documentation:
Access the set of variables that were touched by the most recently generated cube.
This set of variables can be used as a starting point for additional cubes.
The idea is that variables that appear in clauses that are reduced by the most recent
cube are likely more useful to cube on.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Solver.statistics
documentation:

```pycon

>>> x = Int('x')

>>> s.check()

1

True

True
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Optimize.assert_and_track
documentation:
Assert constraint `a` and track it in the unsat core using the Boolean constant `p`.

If `p` is a string, it will be automatically converted into a Boolean constant.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> p3 = Bool('p3')
>>> s = Optimize()
>>> s.assert_and_track(x > 0,  'p1')
>>> s.assert_and_track(x != 1, 'p2')
>>> s.assert_and_track(x < 0,  p3)
>>> print(s.check())
unsat
>>> c = s.unsat_core()
>>> len(c)
2
>>> Bool('p1') in c
True
>>> Bool('p2') in c
False
>>> p3 in c
True
```

Add soft constraint with optional weight and optional identifier.
If no weight is supplied, then the penalty for violating the soft constraint
is 1.
Soft constraints are grouped by identifiers. Soft constraints that are
added without identifiers are grouped by default.

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ApplyResult

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ApplyResult.__len__
documentation:
Return the number of subgoals in `self`.

```pycon
>>> a, b = Ints('a b')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(Or(a == 0, a == 1), Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b)
>>> t = Tactic('split-clause')
>>> r = t(g)
>>> len(r)
2
>>> t = Then(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('split-clause'))
>>> len(t(g))
4
>>> t = Then(Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('split-clause'), Tactic('propagate-values'))
>>> len(t(g))
1
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ApplyResult.__getitem__
documentation:
Return one of the subgoals stored in ApplyResult object `self`.

```pycon
>>> a, b = Ints('a b')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(Or(a == 0, a == 1), Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b)
>>> t = Tactic('split-clause')
>>> r = t(g)
>>> r[0]
[a == 0, Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b]
>>> r[1]
[a == 1, Or(b == 0, b == 1), a > b]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ApplyResult.sexpr
documentation: Return a textual representation of the s-expression representing the set of subgoals in `self`.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.ApplyResult.as_expr
documentation:
Return a Z3 expression consisting of all subgoals.

```pycon
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 1)
>>> g.add(Or(x == 2, x == 3))
>>> r = Tactic('simplify')(g)
>>> r
[[Not(x <= 1), Or(x == 2, x == 3)]]
>>> r.as_expr()
And(Not(x <= 1), Or(x == 2, x == 3))
>>> r = Tactic('split-clause')(g)
>>> r
[[x > 1, x == 2], [x > 1, x == 3]]
>>> r.as_expr()
Or(And(x > 1, x == 2), And(x > 1, x == 3))
```

An ApplyResult object contains the subgoals produced by a tactic when applied to a goal.
It also contains model and proof converters.
ApplyResult

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic.solver
documentation:
Create a solver using the tactic `self`.

The solver supports the methods `push()` and `pop()`, but it
will always solve each `check()` from scratch.

```pycon
>>> t = Then('simplify', 'nlsat')
>>> s = t.solver()
>>> x = Real('x')
>>> s.add(x**2 == 2, x > 0)
>>> s.check()
sat
>>> s.model()
[x = 1.4142135623?]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic.apply
documentation:
Apply tactic `self` to the given goal or Z3 Boolean expression using the given options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = Tactic('solve-eqs')
>>> t.apply(And(x == 0, y >= x + 1))
[[y >= 1]]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic.__call__
documentation:
Apply tactic `self` to the given goal or Z3 Boolean expression using the given options.

```pycon
>>> x, y = Ints('x y')
>>> t = Tactic('solve-eqs')
>>> t(And(x == 0, y >= x + 1))
[[y >= 1]]
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic.help
documentation: Display a string containing a description of the available options for the `self` tactic.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Tactic.param_descrs
documentation: Return the parameter description set.

Tactics transform, solver and/or simplify sets of constraints (Goal).
A Tactic can be converted into a Solver using the method solver().

Several combinators are available for creating new tactics using the built-in ones:
Then(), OrElse(), FailIf(), Repeat(), When(), Cond().
Tactics

classes.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__lt__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is less than the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') < 10
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__gt__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is greater than the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') > 10
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
0.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__le__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is less than or equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') <= 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__ge__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is greater than or equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') >= 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__eq__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') == 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__ne__
documentation:
Return a probe that evaluates to "true" when the value returned by `self`
is not equal to the value returned by `other`.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size') != 2
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
0.0
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.Probe.__call__
documentation:
Evaluate the probe `self` in the given goal.

```pycon
>>> p = Probe('size')
>>> x = Int('x')
>>> g = Goal()
>>> g.add(x > 0)
>>> g.add(x < 10)
>>> p(g)
2.0
>>> g.add(x < 20)
>>> p(g)
3.0
>>> p = Probe('num-consts')
>>> p(g)
1.0
>>> p = Probe('is-propositional')
>>> p(g)
0.0
>>> p = Probe('is-qflia')
>>> p(g)
1.0
```

Probes are used to inspect a goal (aka problem) and collect information that may be used
to decide which solver and/or preprocessing step will be used.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FPRef.__add__
documentation:
Create the Z3 expression `self + other`.

```pycon
>>> x = FP('x', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> y = FP('y', FPSort(8, 24))
>>> x + y
x + y
>>> (x + y).sort()
FPSort(8, 24)
```

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.FPRef.__radd__

```pycon
1.25*(2**3) + x
```

documentation: Either solver is set or ctx is set.
Propagators that are created through callbacks
to "fresh" inherit the context of that is supplied
as argument to the callback.
This context should not be deleted. It is owned by the solver.

methods.name: core.src.api.python.z3.z3.UserPropagateBase.next_split
documentation: Tell the solver to perform the next split on a given term
If the term is a bit-vector the index idx specifies the index of the Boolean variable being
split on. A phase of true = 1/false = -1/undef = 0 = let solver decide is the last argument.
